title: Starbucks is bringing back the Unicorn Frappuccino (but only for one weekend)
body: Starbucks is trolling us this summer. First, the company announced there'd be a brand-new Bearista Cup to fight over (spoiler: I didn't get mine; most local locations said they only got 10 cups total). Then, they brought back the S'mores Frappuccino only to let us know today (via email) that it'll be disappearing soon. Now, they're making another surprise announcement: The Unicorn Frappuccino is back for one weekend only. SEE ALSO: Starbucks is paying baristas to make TikToks. Expect more brands to follow. According to the press release , it'll be back on the menu for "one magical weekend to close out the summer" starting August 15. If you missed the original hysteria in 2017 or its brief pop-up at Coachella it's essentially a sweet-and-tart mango-flavored blend layered with a tangy blue drizzle, topped with whipped cream, and dusted with pink and blue toppings. (Sounds super natural and fine to eat, right?) Some fans compare it to Tropical Skittles . View this post on Instagram As soon as baristas got a whiff of the comeback, they rushed to Reddit to voice their concerns. Apparently, employees have been predicting the drink's return for months after spotting ingredients like "spirulina" and "sparkle topping" on their inventory lists. If you scroll through the threads, veteran baristas still talk about the 2017 launch like it gave them war flashbacks, recalling days of being grossly understaffed, running out of ingredients on Day One, and leaving their shifts covered in sticky pink and blue powder. "Every barista that worked during this time deserves financial compensation,

title: Astronomers say there isnt a word for this weird new space object
body: Astronomers are homing in on a distant realm where the lines between star, planet, and moon are blurred. Researchers have recently discovered that something appears to be orbiting a brown dwarf in the CD-35 2722 system, about 73 light-years away in space , that defies the usual labels. A brown dwarf is already something of a cosmic maverick too large to be called a planet, too small to be a star. Experts sometimes refer to brown dwarfs as "failed stars" because they're not quite massive enough to sustain hydrogen fusion in their cores. That leaves researchers at a loss for what exactly to call this thing circling it. Because the object orbits a brown dwarf that is orbiting a star, it's kind of an " exomoon ," they said, a satellite of a planet found outside the solar system. "Being the third wheel in this system makes us want to call it a moon, even if it is nothing like the small, rocky moons we have in our system," said Kevin Hoy, lead author of the study published in Nature , in a statement . SEE ALSO: Webb telescope found the missing architect of another star system If confirmed, the research team believes this could be the first documented exomoon. But it's nothing like typical moons. The new object, which the team is calling an "exosatellite" for now, is roughly the weight of Jupiter , with the brown dwarf, known as CD-35 2722 B, more than 30 times more massive. A brown dwarf is too large to be called a planet, but too small to be a star and make its own nuclear power. Credit: NASA illustration At that scale, it's likely a gas giant, not rocky. title: Everything announced at Samsung Galaxy Unpacked, including Z Fold 8, Z Flip 8, and smart glasses
body: Samsung held its highly anticipated Galaxy Unpacked event in London on Wednesday morning. The showcase featured three new foldable phones and two new smartwatches, plus more. Spider-Man even made an appearance . Here's everything you need to know from the latest Galaxy Unpacked livestream, plus a first look at the devices. SEE ALSO: Spider-Man swung through Samsung Galaxy Unpacked Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is the cheapest and most fashionable new foldable Cute little guy. Credit: Rene Ramos/Mashable The most affordable foldable Samsung's new lineup is also undeniably the cutest. The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is a fairly straightforward successor to last year's Z Flip 8; it's still a clamshell-style foldable with a small 4.1-inch outer display that mostly exists for checking notifications, and a bigger 6.9-inch inner display where you'll do most of your serious business. It starts at $1,199. The color options are pink, cream, graphite, and mint. Most of the changes from last year are fairly modest. The new Z Flip is about 12 percent smaller than the Z Flip 7 was, and 8g lighter. All three of these new foldables feature Flex Titanium technology, which is intended to make the folding hinges both more durable and less visible to the naked eye. Samsung kept most of the core specs, like RAM, storage, battery size, and camera megapixel counts the same this year. Perhaps the biggest improvement is the presence of a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy chipset, the same as the other two new foldables. title: Samsung Galaxy Watches now detect when youre going to get sick — but its not the first health tracker to do so
body: In London during Galaxy Unpacked 2026 , Samsung debuted new foldable phones and a new generation of smartwatches . On those new watches, the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2 , there's a whole host of new health insights, including sleep apnea detection. But the new Vitals feature on Samsung Galaxy Watches knows when you're getting sick before you do. Using five different biometric markers heart rate, heart rate variability, respiratory rate, skin temperature, and blood oxygen saturation Vitals creates a health baseline for users and detects when those baselines deviate. Users then get an alert that something's off, so you can note when you might be getting sick or need some extra recovery time. Samsung's Vitals feature use five different health biometrics to detect for symptoms. Credit: Samsung While this feature is new to Samsung Galaxy Watches, it's not new in the world of health trackers . Oura Ring , the leader in smart rings, has its own Vitals page, the hub for all your health biometrics. Within Vitals is Symptom Radar , a feature that tracks your biometrics, detects any signs of strain, and encourages you to make daily decisions based on how you feel. Samsung's Vitals seems to be the brand's own version of Symptom Radar. The new batch of Galaxy Watches, including the Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2, comes with boosted battery life for round-the-clock wear. That means they'll always be monitoring your biometrics so that you can have deeper health insights and access to features like Vitals. The new Samsung Galaxy Watches are now up for preorder and will be available on A

title: What happens when you try to chop a photon in half?
author: John Timmer
body: A photon is a single particle of light, and under normal circumstances, it can't be divided. But a photon is also not a particle, in the sense that it does not have a specific location. Instead, it is an extended object. So if a photon is only partway through the process of reflecting from a perfect mirror and you yank the mirror away, what happens? The answer, from a trio of Norwegian physicists, turns out ( arxiv.org link ) to be more complex than I expected. A photon divided? Let’s first talk briefly about dividing and combining photons. If this were a common experience in our lives, then shining a single color of light through a piece of glass or reflecting it from a surface might cause photons to divide or combine. This would lead to an amazing array of colors: Our universe would be the most fantastic and legal LSD trip you could imagine. But this doesn't generally happen, hence LSD. Read full article Comments

title: Spider-Man swung through Samsung Galaxy Unpacked
body: Partway through today's Samsung Galaxy Unpacked , a familiar face swung through: Spider-Man. Ahead of the release of Spider-Man: Brand New Day at the end of July, Samsung played a video of all the phones the superhero breaks while trying to save New York City. But, thankfully, the new Samsung foldable phone is apparently much more durable. SEE ALSO: I tried Samsung's new foldables. They have a cool factor the iPhone can't match. During the video, however, Spider-Man is suspiciously silent, leading viewers to think that the company couldn't get actor Tom Holland to appear. But no matter, his friend Ned Leeds (Jacob Batalon) was able to show off the new phone in his stead. Jacob Batalon even made an appearance on the Unpacked Stage in London to show off the phone. Credit: Mashable And Spider-Man (OK, some guy in a Spider-Man suit) even popped in to close out the segment.

title: Samsung unveils new Galaxy Z8 foldables and Galaxy Watches, available August 7
author: Ryan Whitwam
body: The rumors haven't left much to the imagination, but Samsung has unfurled its 2026 foldable lineup today. There are three models for the first time in Samsung's eight-gen foldable family, including the top-of-the-line Z Fold 8 Ultra. That phone is the successor to last year's Galaxy Z Fold 7 , but the Z Fold 8 is new—the company's first wide-body foldable designed for media over multitasking. And then there's a new, thinner flip phone, plus a pair of smartwatches. You can preorder Samsung's new hardware today, but the component shortage has made these already spendy product lines more expensive this year. Even the Z Flip 8 is now well over a grand, and the Ultra costs almost twice as much. title: T-Mobile is offering a free Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 — heres how this preorder deal works
body: TL;DR: T-Mobile is celebrating Samsung Unpacked by offering the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 for free saving you $379.99 (100% off) via 24 monthly bill credits when you add a qualifying wearable line. Opens in a new window Credit: Samsung Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 Free via 24 monthly bill credits when you add a qualifying wearable line. Shop Now Carrier launch windows are historically the only time you can score 100% off a newly-announced flagship smartwatch without trading in an older device. With Samsung Unpacked officially underway, T-Mobile is attempting to lock in subscribers by wiping away the entire $379.99 retail cost of the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 . If you've been holding out on adding a cellular smartwatch to your wrist setup, this is the lowest upfront price you will see all year. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group As savvy consumers, you should know that carrier bill credits always come with strings attached. While wiping out the Galaxy Watch 9's $379.99 sticker price upfront sounds like a steal, you aren't walking away without spending anything. To get this new smartwatch for $0 , T-Mobile requires you to add a new paired wearable line to your monthly account. Assuming you have AutoPay turned on, that service line will run you around $12 to $15 per month. Add in T-Mobile's standard one-time $35 connection charge at checkout, and your actual out-of-pocket cost over the 24-month contract cycle comes to roughly $323 (plus tax). If you genuinely want true, phone-free independence, paying around $323 over two years for a top-tier $380 smartwatch plus two full years of stan

title: Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
author: WIRED
body: A little over a month after the Department of Defense (DOD) bragged that nearly half of its 3.5 million employees were using AI at work, members of the Army’s Combat Capabilities Development Command (DEVCOM) received an email informing them that they were burning through tokens, and needed to limit use. “Although the Army CIO announced in May 2026 that they were offering unlimited tokens, by mid-June the Army CIO pool was exhausted of tokens and had to re-establish limits,” the email reads. The email goes on to say that although the Army has chosen to renew token usage at “its current levels,” it’s unclear “if the Army CIO pool will be renewed after 1 Oct.” The Army uses Ask Sage , a multimodal generative AI platform where users can run different large language models (LLMs), including Alphabet’s Gemini, Meta’s Llama, and OpenAI’s ChatGPT. “Apparently the whole Army burned through the whole year of tokens for just one service,” says an Army employee who spoke to WIRED anonymously because they were not authorized to speak to the press. Read full article Comments

title: Samsung Galaxy Glasses shown at Unpacked. See them now.
body: At Samsung Galaxy Unpacked on Tuesday, Samsung provided a closer look at its upcoming smart glasses . Samsung is working with Korean eyewear brand Gentle Monster and Warby Parker on the design, and the glasses will run on Google's new Android XR operating system. SEE ALSO: Samsung reveals undeniably hip Galaxy Z Flip 8 foldable at Galaxy Unpacked in London We first saw these glasses at Google I/O 2026 in May, and there will be at least four designs , according to CNET, with different frame styles. Google has been teasing its smart glasses collaboration with Samsung for months, and previously said the first pair of smart glasses would be released in 2026. During the Galaxy Unpacked event, Samsung gave another peek at the glasses: Credit: Shannon Connellan / Mashable Credit: Samsung According to CNET, these glasses are display-free but have a camera, microphone, and speakers. It supports Gemini AI and can connect with Android phones and Google Wear OS watches. The glasses will run both Google Gemini and Samsung Bixby AI. Samsung's COO of mobile experiences, Won-Joon Choi, told CNET that the glasses can run for 9 hours, while the case can charge them an additional 7 times. The glasses will not have a built-in display, but will have built-in cameras for content capture and microphones to help power AI features. This is a developing story. Watch this space for further updates from Samsung Galaxy Unpacked.

title: T-Mobile is giving away the new Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 for free — how to claim
body: TL;DR: Pre-order the new Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 for free when you trade in an eligible device on an Experience Beyond plan with T-Mobile. Opens in a new window Credit: Samsung Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Free via 24 monthly bill credits with an eligible trade-in on an Experience Beyond plan at T-Mobile Shop Now Samsung Unpacked has officially unveiled the Galaxy Z Fold 8 , and while it's thinner and wider than its predecessor, it carries a hefty $1,899.99 retail price tag. Buying it outright is a massive investment, but there is a crafty hack you should know about. Carrier pre-order windows are traditionally the single best time to bypass upfront hardware costs. T-Mobile's opening promotion offers up to $1,900 in bill credits over 24 months. If you have an eligible flagship to trade in, this completely cancels out the phone's sticker price making it the most aggressive launch offer we've seen for a book-style foldable to date. How to get the Galaxy Z Fold 8 for free Getting the maximum trade-in value on the Fold 8 requires a few specific steps: Trade in: To unlock the full $1,900 credit ceiling, you must trade in an eligible high-end device (such as a Galaxy Z Fold 6/7, S25 Ultra, or recent iPhone Pro Max) in good condition. Select a plan: You must be on, or switch to, T-Mobile s Experience Beyond plan (or the equivalent Go5G Plus/Next tiers). You will still need to pay local state sales tax on the full $1,899.99 retail price upfront at checkout, plus T-Mobile s standard $35 connection charge. Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2: Price and availability Two new watches are coming to the Samsung Galaxy lineup, and only one is getting a price increase from the previous generation. title: Kylian Mbappé is the face of EA Sports FC 27 — heres everything to know before the gameplay reveal
body: TL;DR: Kylian Mbapp is the cover athlete for EA Sports FC 27: Ultimate Edition . The soccer star returns to the franchise cover for the first time in four years ahead of the game's gameplay reveal on July 23. Kylian Mbapp is returning to the cover of EA s football franchise. Electronic Arts has revealed the French superstar as the cover athlete for EA Sports FC 27 Ultimate Edition , marking his first time as an EA Sports FC cover star and his return to the franchise cover after four years. Mbapp will also appear as the new cover athlete for EA Sports FC Mobile , continuing his long-running relationship with the football gaming franchise. The Real Madrid forward earned the cover spot after another standout season on the pitch. Since joining Real Madrid in 2024, Mbapp has finished as La Liga top scorer in both of his seasons in Spain and claimed the 2025/26 UEFA Champions League Golden Boot after scoring 15 goals in the competition. He has also continued to make history with the French national team, becoming the World Cup s all-time leading goalscorer and the first player to win the Golden Boot twice. SEE ALSO: English footballer Wayne Rooney's blunt reaction to World Cup halftime show goes viral To be featured on the cover of an EA Sports FC title for the first time is a very special moment for me, Mbapp said. It's an honor to continue my long-term relationship with the franchise in its latest chapter, alongside the players who have shaped its history. I can't wait for everyone to experience FC 27 . title: I tried Samsungs new foldables. They have a cool factor the iPhone cant match.
body: The new Samsung Galaxy foldables are about to debut July 22 at Galaxy Unpacked. We're excited to stress-test them properly later, but here are my thoughts after playing around with the new Galaxy foldable smartphones ahead of time. We've already covered the launch of the three new foldables the Galaxy Z Flip 8 , the Galaxy Z Fold 8 , and the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra but let's talk about what it's like holding these new phones in your hand. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group My first impressions have been very positive, albeit in the absence of testing processor benchmarks, battery rundowns, or photography. But I did have a lot of fun playing around with these foldables, and that's more than I can say about a lot of regular smartphones. Meet the new Samsung Galaxy foldables Credit: Joseph Maldonado / Mashable Here's how to distinguish between the three members of the Galaxy Z 8 lineup. The Galaxy Z Flip 8 is the trendy, flip phone-style device that fits in your pocket, with a $1,199.99 starting price. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 is a new, wider 4:3 form factor introduced this year. It has a similar size and design to the highly anticipated iPhone Fold Ultra , Apple's rumored foldable we hope to see unveiled in September. Finally, the Z Fold 8 Ultra is the true flagship of the bunch, with a camera array that comes closer to matching the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra released in the spring . The Z Fold 8 is a more mid-range option, with a starting price of $1,899.99, while the Z Fold 8 Ultra has an intimidating $2,199.99 starting price. When unfolded and powered on, you won't see the creas

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 vs. Motorola Razr Ultra
body: The clamshell foldable phone market is a crowded space these days, thanks in part to the efforts of Motorola and Samsung . Motorola released its Razr Ultra earlier this year, while Samsung is set to reveal the Z Flip 8 at its Galaxy Unpacked event on July 22. We'll do our best to compare and contrast. Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 vs. Motorola Razr Ultra: Price The Razr Ultra retails for $1,499. While we don't know the price for the Z Flip 8 yet, the Z Flip 7 launched at $1,099 last year. Given the price-rising RAMageddon in the tech world right now, a price increase of some kind seems likely. The Z Flip 8 may still come in below $1,499, but you can pretty much guarantee it won't be below $1,099. Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 vs. Motorola Razr Ultra: Design The Razr Ultra and Z Flip 8 will almost certainly look pretty similar at a glance. Credit: Ren Ramos / Mashable In terms of physical design, both of these phones are very similar. They're both clamshell-style foldables with a small outer display and a larger inner display that you use while unfolded. The Razr Ultra has a 7-inch inner display and a 4-inch outer display, while leaks indicate the Flip 8's displays will be 6.9-inches and 4.1-inches, respectively, meaning the difference in display size between these two phones is nearly nonexistent. Size is where Samsung might have an advantage. Here are the dimensions of the Razr Ultra: Folded: 88.12 73.99 15.69 mm Unfolded: 171.48 73.99 7.19 mm Weight: 199g We don't have an official spec sheet for the Z Flip 8 yet, but all reports suggest it will have a similar build to the Z Flip 7, p

title: Samsung announces Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2, with new AI features
body: Samsung didn't bring back all of its Galaxy Watch models for 2026, but it did just reveal two new ones. At its Galaxy Unpacked showcase on Wednesday, Samsung announced the new Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. And it turns out pre-show rumors of no new Galaxy Watch Classic this year were correct. The Galaxy Watch 9 starts at $379.99 for Bluetooth and $429.99 for the LTE model. Meanwhile, the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 starts at a substantially more expensive $699.99. Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2 specs Watch out. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group The Galaxy Watch 9, Samsung's successor to the Galaxy Watch 8 , will come in 40mm and 44mm models, while the Ultra 2 is sized at 47mm. Samsung upgraded the battery in the larger Watch 9 this year, giving it a 445mAh cell, but the 40mm variant only has a 390mAh cell in it. The biggest battery improvement comes on the Watch Ultra 2, which will come with a whopping 800mAh cell. Other specs include: Watch 9: 2GB memory Watch Ultra 2: 2GB memory Watch 9: 32GB storage Watch Ultra 2: 64GB storage Both watches: Snapdragon Wear Elite chip The Ultra 2 also comes with a very bright 5,000 nits of peak brightness in its display, making it ideal for outdoor usage. On the Watch 9, you get just 3,000 nits, which should still be good enough for most occasions. title: Samsung’s newest foldable finally feels Ultra
body: While we wait for Apple's rumored foldable iPhone , Samsung is polishing a design it already has on the market. I just spent three hours with my hands on the new Z Fold 8 Ultra, and all I can think about is how Samsung finally made the crease invisible. Samsung says it achieved this thanks to its new Flex Titanium display tech. Its foldable screens now utilize a titanium film that sits below the OLED panel, which Samsung says has 20-times the mechanical stiffness as opposed to the polymer film it used in its old foldables. This film is bonded to a new titanium plate, and when used together, the crease that used to plague foldable devices is … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Samsung’s wider Z Fold 8 feels just right
body: The new Z Fold 8 is squat and wide, but don’t hold that against it. | Photo: David Imel / The Verge A year after overhauling its Z Fold phone with a radically thinner design, Samsung has changed things up again, this time altering the shape entirely. The Z Fold 8 is wider than any of Samsung's previous foldables, and shorter too, a size and shape somewhere between Samsung's traditional Flip and Fold models. In photos, I wasn't impressed by the squat shape. In person, to my surprise, it feels great, and almost immediately intuitive. If you want to get a feel for it yourself, grab your passport - the Z Fold 8 is about the same height and less than half an inch thinner. This isn't Samsung's only new foldable this year, and in fact, its line … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2 bet big on battery
body: The squircle squad is here to stay. | Photo: David Imel / The Verge It's a year of refinement for the Galaxy Watch. With the new Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, which are being announced today, Samsung is more or less taking what people like about its watches and tweaking those elements to be better. The company has been doing this for the past few generations of its watch line, but this year's updates seem focused on shoring up Samsung's wearable weaknesses. Namely, battery life and preventive health features. The Ultra 2, now $50 more expensive at $699.99, gets a few key spec bumps. The 47mm watch packs in a whopping 800mAh battery - a major 35 percent increase from the 590mAh battery in the orig … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: I almost forgot Samsung’s Z Flip 8 was a foldable
body: Samsung's new Galaxy Z Flip 8 feels more like a regular phone than ever. It's thinner, lighter, and has a front screen you can do just about anything on. Whether that's a good or bad thing, I'm not quite sure yet. I spent three hours with Samsung's newest flip phone last week, and it doesn't look too different from last year's Z Flip 7 . It's still got a 6.9-inch FHD+ AMOLED display on the inside. It's still got a 4.1-inch Super AMOLED cover screen on the outside. It still starts at 12GB of RAM and 256GB of storage, and it still has a 4,300mAh battery. But this year Samsung has added even more features that make this thing a full-on phone w … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Pete Hegseth is a dangerous failure
author: Max Burns, opinion contributor
body: When Pete Hegseth stepped into the role of Defense secretary in January 2025, the former Fox News host defined his own terms for success: “Warfighting, lethality, meritocracy, standards, and readiness. title: Samsung finally reveals the Galaxy Z Fold 8 that will compete with the iPhone Ultra
body: After months of buildup, Samsung finally unveiled its newest, most interesting foldable device the Galaxy Z Fold 8. At a Galaxy Unpacked event in London, the Korean tech company announced three new foldable phones: the Galaxy Z Flip 8 , the Galaxy Z Fold 8, and the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra . However, it's the Z Fold 8 that might be the biggest draw, for two reasons. First, the Z Fold 8 is an entirely new device and form factor. Second, it looks well-placed to compete head-to-head with the still-unannounced iPhone Ultra Fold, which we think will be revealed in September. We expect Apple's first foldable to have a shorter, wider form factor, just like the Z Fold 8. Mashable got the chance to try the new Galaxy foldables ahead of the launch event, but before we get into the details, we have one point of clarification. Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 is not really a successor to the Z Fold 7 from a year ago, as you might expect. If you want that, check out the new Z Fold 8 Ultra . The all-new Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 is available for pre-order today and starts at $1,899.99. It's available in four colors lavender, cream, graphite, and pistachio and will be widely available starting Aug. 7. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 specs The Galaxy Z Fold 8 has a sizable inner display. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group The new Galaxy Z Fold 8 foldable smartphone has a 4:3 aspect ratio, which is an ideal size whether in landscape or portrait orientation. The inner display has enough extra real estate for multitasking and entertainment. And if you're worried about a crease in the middle of the display, do

title: Samsung reveals undeniably hip Galaxy Z Flip 8 foldable at Galaxy Unpacked in London
body: All eyes were on Samsung's two bigger foldable devices at Unpacked on Wednesday, but the newest in the Galaxy Z Flip line got its moment in the spotlight, too. Samsung confirmed the long-known existence of the Galaxy Z Flip 8 at an Unpacked event in London , following up on last year's well-liked Z Flip 7 with a device that offers a smaller form factor, but at a higher price. The new Z Flip 8 is just 6.1 mm when unfolded, making it 13 percent thinner than the Z Flip 7. That's all well and good, but unfortunately, like almost everything else in the tech world, it's more expensive than last year's model, too. The new Galaxy Z Flip 8 is available for preorder today starting at $1,199.99. That's a $100 price bump year-over-year. The new foldable phone will be available on Aug. 7, but we got the chance to try the device ahead of its launch, and we've got all the details you need to know. Opens in a new window Credit: Samsung Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 $1,199.99 at Samsung Pre-order Here Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 specs Credit: Joseph Maldonado / Mashable The Z Flip 8 is noticeably lighter and slimmer in your hand this year, and Samsung says it's the lightest and thinnest flip-style foldable phone ever . Having seen the device up close, we believe it. This year's device weighs a mere 180 grams, or 8 grams lighter than the Z Flip 7 . Here are the basic specs of the new Galaxy Z Flip 8 from Samsung: Inner display: 6.9-inch FHD+ AMOLED display with 120Hz refresh rate Outer display: 4.1-inch HD+ AMOLED display with 120Hz refresh rate Processor: Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy chipset C

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is the new king of the foldables, but it will cost you
body: After months of rumors and leaks, Samsung finally told us everything we wanted to know about its new marquee foldable smartphone, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra was officially announced during a Galaxy Unpacked showcase in London, where the company also debuted two more foldable phones and two new Galaxy smartwatches . In a twist to its usual naming convention, the Z Fold 8 Ultra is the successor to last year's excellent Z Fold 7 . That means it's a new book-style foldable with some hardware and software improvements over the Z Fold 7. (The newly announced Z Fold 8 is an entirely new model with a wider body.) In keeping with the Ultra name, and the general state of the tech industry, the Z Fold 8 Ultra is also the most expensive of the new foldables announced on Wednesday. It starts at a staggering $2,099.99, a $100 price increase over the Z Fold 7. Mashable got the chance to demo the new foldable smartphones ahead of their release, and we can finally share all the details and our first impressions. The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra: By the numbers An Ultra display. Credit: Joseph Maldonado / CNET Group This year, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 rumors and leaks were pretty spot on. As expected, the new device features a new Qualcomm Snapdragon chipset (the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy), an improved battery, and some new AI features. Remarkable, it's even thinner than last year's model. title: OpenAI agent went rogue, escaped, and hacked Hugging Face
body: On Tuesday, OpenAI published a blog post with a fairly unassuming name: "OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation." Once you dig in, it reads like a cyberpunk novel in which OpenAI created an advanced AI hacker agent and put it in an isolated environment for testing. The AI agent then autonomously hacked its way out of OpenAI's isolated environment and then hacked Hugging Face, an open-source repository of AI-related projects, to find a solution to an advanced test designed to evaluate an AI's hacking abilities. In other words, the AI proved that it is, indeed, really good at hacking things. But this is exactly the stuff of AI nightmares that cybersecurity experts have been warning us about , and it could have serious ramifications for OpenAI. The story starts with Hugging Face, the AI repository (think of it as GitHub for AI), which disclosed a security incident last week . In a blog post, the company said it recently detected an intrusion into its infrastructure that was "different from anything we had handled" as it was completely driven by an "autonomous AI agent system." The AI, according to Hugging Face, exploited a chain of vulnerabilities to obtain credentials and access data from its servers. It did so by "executing many thousands of individual actions across a swarm of short-lived sandboxes, with self-migrating command-and-control staged on public services." Sounds scary, but it gets worse. SEE ALSO: The 6 biggest cybersecurity breaches of 2026 so far OpenAI's part of the story exposes just how dangerous this thing is. T

title: Ukrainian drones deliver robots directly into battle by sea and air
author: Jeremy Hsu
body: In the latest twist on battlefield innovations, Ukraine has used both flying drone carriers and drone boats to send ground robots armed with weapons or explosives into battle. The drone-assisted deployments have enabled slower wheeled robots to bypass tough terrain and Russian defenses, and to carry out an unusual robotic amphibious assault on a contested position. Such tactics also showcase the Ukrainian military’s ongoing experimentation with using drones and robots to offset a Russian military advantage in manpower while also sparing Ukrainian soldiers from making dangerous assaults. On July 19, a Russian military Telegram channel posted a video showing a Ukrainian hexacopter drone carrying a ground robot suspended below by a cable, according to the Ukrainian government-run news platform United24 . The Russian Telegram channel described the Ukrainian heavy bomber drone as deploying a four-wheeled ARK-1 robot armed with explosive anti-tank mines on the ground where it continued toward Russian military positions—and apparently generated plenty of outraged comments from pro-Kremlin social media users. But it was basically a footnote buried in the company's announcement for its Muse image and video generation tools. title: The 2026 Honda Prelude is a marvel of hybrid technology
body: When it comes to enthusiast-geared Honda hardware, the Civic Si, Civic Type R, and Acura NSX often come to mind first for their revvy VTEC (Honda's form of variable valve timing) engines and playful chassis dynamics. The Prelude, on the other hand, less so. Instead, the Prelude is a technological study - still aimed at driving fun, but by way of integrating clever forms of engineering into a sleek grand touring coupe body. The second-generation Prelude's fuel injection was derived from F1. Then the third gen got four-wheel steering way back in the late '80s. The fourth gen? If you just want to be told today's word, you can jump to the bottom of this article for today's Wordle solution revealed. But if you'd rather solve it yourself, keep reading for some clues, tips, and strategies to assist you. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 22, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? title: Buy $15 of Takis and score $8 towards a Spider-Man: Brand New Day ticket — everything you need to know
body: TL;DR: Ahead of Spider-Man: Brand New Day 's July 31 theatrical debut, Takis has launched limited-edition collectible bags inspired by the film. From July 1 through Aug. 31, shoppers who spend $15 on qualifying Takis products can earn $8 in Fandango Rewards for a movie ticket. Fans counting down to Spider-Man: Brand New Day can earn a discount on their movie ticket with their next snack run. Takis has launched limited-edition bags inspired by the upcoming Marvel film, and shoppers who spend $15 on participating products through Aug. 31 will receive $8 in Fandango Rewards that can be used towards a ticket to see Spider-Man: Brand New Day . Takis has rolled out limited-edition Spider-Man: Brand New Day -themed packaging across four of its rolled tortilla chip flavors: Fuego, Blue Heat, Intense Nacho, and Nitro. Each flavor sports a different collectible design, and fans can find the bags at participating retailers nationwide. The limited-edition packaging and ticket offer are just part of the collaboration. Takis says it will also bring the partnership to life with fan experiences, influencer content, and in-store promotions throughout the summer. Spider-Man: Brand New Day swings exclusively into theaters on July 31, starring Tom Holland, Zendaya, Sadie Sink, Jacob Batalon, Jon Bernthal, Tramell Tillman, Michael Mando, and Mark Ruffalo. It s not quite web-slinging, but saving $8 on a movie ticket is a pretty good superpower for a bag of chips.

title: The Daily Show reacts to Trump slapping additional tariffs on Canada
body: The word "tariff" seems to have been blissfully absent from the news cycle for a while now, but this week it made a comeback. In the Daily Show clip above, host Desi Lydic reacts to Trump announcing 50 percent tariffs on several goods imported from Canada , from dairy products and paper to furniture and hockey sticks. "No hockey sticks? What's my husband gonna keep jammed in the corner of our garage for the past 15 years?" Lydic said. "Now, obviously, it's very frustrating to be back in this trade war again, but if you're worried that this is another case of Trump abusing his power again relax, these tariffs are legal under the Smoot-Hawley act, which has been around since 1930 when it helped prolong and worse the great depression. So cheer up!" Lydic goes on to explain why the tariffs are being imposed now, citing a Truth Social post in which Trump accuses Canada of "not properly maintaining their forests," leading to the US "being unnecessarily invaded by filthy, polluted, and unhealthy air." "Are you sure you want America to set a precedent for paying for pollution?" asks Lydic. "I feel like your pals at Pesticide-Microplastic-Death-Diarrhea-Lettuce Inc. might have other ideas."

title: What to look for when buying a TV, as told through this uncomplicated TV specs guide
body: Recommendations for the best TVs to buy in 2026 are at the bottom of this guide, thanks to intel from CNET's Ty Pendlebury and PCMag's Will Greenwald . (CNET, PCMag, and Mashable are all owned by publisher Ziff Davis.) Use the table of contents to skip down to a specific category. Buying a new television can break your brain more than it breaks your budget. If you're not a picky cinephile or longtime hardcore gamer , TV specs are one of those Michael Scott "Explain it to me like I'm five" things. Laptop specs are in the same club. Wordy product titles on Amazon TVs definitely aren't helping the confusion. The clusters of nebulous descriptors like "Omni Mini-LED, QLED 4K UHD, Dolby Vision IQ, 144hz, Ambient Experience" aren't exactly accessible to the average person they're just pure chaos. Familiarizing yourself with all of this technical stuff doesn't require a lab or any prior knowledge at all. It might just take a straightforward TV buyers guide, broken down by someone who's used to feeling overwhelmed by tech jargon. So, here's your brain workout for the day: Let's translate all of these TV acronyms and buzzwords so that you know exactly what to look for when buying a new TV. SEE ALSO: What is the slimmest TV you can buy? I compared numbers to find the thinnest OLED, QLED, and RGB TVs. Backlight explained: LED vs. QLED vs. OLED vs. RGB The main thing to know: 4K and LED are not talking about the same thing. 4K refers to resolution, and terms containing -LED refer to the panel lighting the TV. title: How to watch the Samsung Galaxy Unpacked livestream: See the new phones debut live
body: The next Samsung Galaxy Unpacked is finally here. The launch event will be held in London on Wednesday, July 22 at 9 a.m. ET , and Samsung will almost certainly unveil a slew of new products. Samsung has already confirmed that new details on its next generation of foldable smartphones will be announced. The tagline for this year's event is "A new shape unfolds," which isn't exactly subtle, and we're fully expecting to see the Galaxy Z Flip 8 and Galaxy Z Fold 8 take center stage. Will Samsung's new Fold and Flip smartphones cost more , as the leaks have pointed to? Is the highly anticipated Galaxy Z Fold Ultra going to be worth the premium price tag? Most importantly, does Samsung have any surprises up its sleeve? We might be too optimistic, but a tease of the unreleased Galaxy Glasses isn't out of the question. Here's how you can watch the event live. Watch Galaxy Unpacked live on YouTube Samsung Galaxy Unpacked will livestream right on the company's website and on YouTube . Don't forget: Because the event takes place in the United Kingdom, we're getting an early start time this year for viewers in the United States. The event will kick off at 9 a.m. ET (6 a.m. PT). And, of course, Mashable will be providing real-time coverage and analysis before, during, and immediately after the event. We also have reporters attending the London launch event, so keep checking back for more live updates and previews of all the new devices, including the Galaxy Z Flip 8, Galaxy Z Fold 8, Galaxy Watch 9, and the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 (if the rumors are true, that is).

title: What happened on the JoJo Siwa Cruise? Fans describe the weekend at sea
body: For three days in June, JoJo Siwa fans could do more than watch her perform. They could go on vacation with her. The JoJo Siwa Cruise departed from Port Canaveral, Florida, on June 12 for a three-night sailing aboard Royal Caribbean's Utopia of the Seas, where a regular cruise itinerary became a floating fan weekend: rhinestone bow decorating, a dance class, nightly parties hosted by Siwa, a cabana beach day, and a concert at sea. Siwa, the singer, dancer, internet personality, and former Dance Moms star, first became famous as a child performer known for her oversized bows, high-energy music, and brightly colored brand. In the years since, she has grown up in public, moving from children's entertainment into a more adult pop era that has included new music, reality TV appearances, and a fanbase that now includes both younger viewers and adults who have followed her for years. The cruise reflected that broad fanbase. A group of fans who self-identify on Instagram as "The Divas" says the people in their group who attended range in age from 9 to 49. They became bigger fans of Siwa through Celebrity Big Brother and saw the cruise promoted on her social media. "There were so many events and activities," the group tells Mashable, listing bow decorating, dance class, hibachi dinner, a cabana beach day, nightly parties, and the concert. "The concert show she performed too, which was just phenomenal." "She'd worked on new choreography especially for the weekend, so it was really exciting to experience that," Alice, a 32-year-old fan who runs the Joelle Fans UK account, says. View t

title: How to delete your AdultFriendFinder account
body: There are so many reasons to delete an online dating profile. It might be because you've found that special somebody already and you want to show commitment by officially leaving the dating landscape. Perhaps you're just happy to remain as a singleton . Or maybe you just want a break from all swipes, matches, and "heys." Thankfully, AdultFriendFinder is one of the hookup platforms that make it super easy to delete your account and permanently scrub your profile data. First, understand that deleting your account is different from canceling your Gold membership . If you want to continue using AdultFriendFinder for free, or just want to stop your monthly payments, you can just cancel the subscription without deleting your entire account. Actual account deletion is a more permanent action: you ll lose all of your profile info, all of your chat history, and all of your settings. So, be sure before you go ahead consider whether you 100 percent want to wipe all that information. Particularly if you're a longtime user with a lot of history on the site. SEE ALSO: The best sexting apps for discreet NSFW chats If you do want to go ahead, you can choose whether to delete your account via a web browser or on your phone. The steps are different, but the end result is the same. title: Substack adds tool that detects AI-generated content
body: Substack is introducing a new tool to help readers figure out whether content was written by a human or generated by AI. Announced in a blog post on Tuesday, the self-publishing platform's new feature is powered by AI detection tool Pangram , and is available on Substack posts, notes, replies, and comments. SEE ALSO: Substack gives creators control of the conversation with new Reply Rules "Not everything made with AI is slop, and not all slop is made with AI," wrote Substack co-founder and CEO Chris Best. "The problem is when there is a mismatch between a reader s expectation and reality, especially when they unwittingly invest their attention in something with no human thought on the other end." Substack's AI detection tool is available now on web and in the iOS app, with Android to follow. To scan Substack content for AI contributions, simply click or tap on the "..." meatball menu, then select "Scan for AI text." A popup will then appear stating what percentage of the text Pangram has determined is AI-generated, AI-assisted, or human. Substack's new AI detection feature does have some restrictions. Specifically, it will only work on Substack text passages longer than 100 words, and only those published after 8:30 a.m. PT / 11:30 a.m. on July 21. Attempting to scan Substack content published prior to this results in a popup stating that it's ineligible for AI detection. Trying to check passages that are shorter than 100 words also surfaces a popup explaining that there isn't enough text for accurate AI detection. The popup on Best's own posts also offers an additional mes

title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 22, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. title: NYT Pips hints, answers for July 22, 2026
body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 22, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

title: NYT Strands hints, answers for July 22, 2026
body: Today's NYT Strands hints are easy if you're a trivia lover. Strands , the New York Times ' elevated word-search game, requires the player to perform a twist on the classic word search. Words can be made from linked letters up, down, left, right, or diagonal, but words can also change direction, resulting in quirky shapes and patterns. Every single letter in the grid will be part of an answer. There's always a theme linking every solution, along with the "spangram," a special, word or phrase that sums up that day's theme, and spans the entire grid horizontally or vertically. title: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Range Rover provided flights from Washington, DC, to London and accommodation so Ars could see the RR GT, as well as some other things you can read about in early September. Ars does not accept paid editorial content. WOLVERHAMPTON, England—One can argue about whether or not Range Rover invented the luxury SUV category, but there's no question that since the first Range Rover went on sale in 1970, the brand has made itself the standard that other luxury SUVs have to measure against. But what if Range Rover built something that wasn't an SUV? That's exactly what it's doing with the new Range Rover GT. Spy shots have been circulating for at least a year of what was believed to be an all-electric replacement for the Range Rover Velar . It turns out that was a misapprehension; the heavily camouflaged prototypes were examples of the Range Rover GT being tested at the Nürburgring and the Arctic Circle . Will this be Range Rover's answer to the Lucid Air and Porsche Taycan? Credit: Range Rover Range Rover still isn't ready to reveal an uncamouflaged GT, but the car you see is much less disguised than the boxy-looking prototypes seen in earlier spy shots. Read full article Comments

The expanded autonomous surveillance plan , including tech upgrades to existing towers overseen by DHS, is estimated to cost around $1 billion and aims to be finished by 2034. Upon completion, the country's borders will boast 2,300 surveillance towers it currently operates around 830, the Electronic Frontier Foundation reports. SEE ALSO: Google's AI Search is a minefield for schools The expenditure is part of the Customs and Border Protection's (CBP) consolidated Integrated Surveillance Tower (IST) program, which operates a network of intel-gathering towers and surveillance that detect "items of interest" along the border and in nearby towns. The towers are integrated into a central system accessible by agents. "It s not just a barrier. A lot of people miss that [it] has technology built into it," said CBP Commissioner Rodney Scott during an April budget hearing with lawmakers regarding the agency's plans. Building on years of work to outfit border regions with technologically-advanced security systems, the White House has proposed millions in infrastructure for a "smart" border wall , including autonomous and AI-powered surveillance towers that alert agents to potential security threats. In 2025 and 2026, more than $240 million was allocated for the IST program. The 2027 budget steered another $97 million toward the construction of 95 new towers over the next year.

author: Beth Mole
body: One of Rhode Island's most-wanted fugitives was arrested in New York last week after being on the run for more than 20 years, which was at least partially spent hiding as a biotech executive, according to Stat News . In a Securities and Exchange Commission filing Monday, California-based Immix Biopharma noted that Richard Graydon was terminated Friday for " reasons unrelated to his activities at the Company ." It turns out, Richard Graydon was an alias for Ronald Fischer, a 70-year-old former anesthesiologist licensed in Rhode Island and Massachusetts, who fled amid his 2005 trial for first-degree sexual assault. title: Spotify launches its first global music video chart
body: Spotify is giving music videos their own Top 50. On Tuesday, July 21, Spotify launched its first global chart dedicated to music videos, ranking the 50 most-watched videos on the platform each day. Artists can also pitch new videos directly to Spotify's editorial team for the first time, giving them a chance to appear on playlists including Best New Videos, Live Performances, and Today's Top Videos. The features arrive after a steady expansion of music videos across Spotify, including full-length uploads, personalized recommendations, and early premieres from artists like BTS , Jelly Roll, and Taylor Swift . Artists can also now upload their own videos through Spotify for Artists. SEE ALSO: Spotify adds editor videos to New Music Friday. We asked its curators why. Add it all together, and Spotify is beginning to resemble a version of MTV built for the streaming era, complete with exclusive premieres, countdowns, and ever-changing rankings. There may not be a VJ telling viewers what comes next, but there is an AI DJ if you're into that sort of thing. Otherwise, Spotify's editors and recommendation algorithms fill the role. Spotify has built its own music video countdown The new Music Video Charts Global ranks videos by daily stream counts, refreshes daily at 6 p.m. ET, and lives in Spotify's Charts and Music Video Playlists hubs. Every Tuesday, the company will also highlight the week s top 10 videos across its social media accounts. Shakira and Burna Boy claimed the chart s inaugural No. 1 with "Dai Dai," the official song of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which just surpassed 13

On July 16th, Hugging Face disclosed a security incident that it says was driven by "an autonomous AI agent system." title: Judge approves Anthropics $1.5 billion settlement with authors
body: A federal judge in San Francisco has granted final approval to a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and a class of authors who accused the AI company of illegally downloading their copyrighted books, closing out what the court called the largest copyright class action settlement in history. U.S. District Judge Araceli Mart nez-Olgu n signed the order on July 20 , resolving claims brought by lead plaintiffs Andrea Bartz, Kirk Wallace Johnson, and others over Anthropic's use of pirated libraries LibGen and PiLiMi to build its book collection. Notably, the underlying dispute centered on how Anthropic acquired the books, not on whether training AI models with copyrighted material is lawful. SEE ALSO: Anthropic to provide California government with AI tools In an earlier ruling cited throughout the order , the court had already determined that the use of training itself constituted fair use. Per the settlement, authors and publishers whose pirated books ended up on Anthropic's "Works List" can claim roughly $3,000 per book about four times the usual minimum for copyright infringement cases. More than 91 percent of eligible works, over 440,000 books, have already been claimed. Anthropic also has to delete the pirated files it downloaded. The settlement only releases Anthropic from liability for how it acquired the training data in the past not from future lawsuits over what the chatbot generates, or new claims going forward. Judge Araceli Mart nez-Olgu n was explicit that the settlement does not release claims for future harm or claims "based on the output of AI models." T

title: The NWSL is finding its next generation of fans online
body: The National Women's Soccer League (NWSL) is finding new fans in more places than the stadium. According to new data from the league , views across its YouTube channels increased 41.7 percent through the first 10 weeks of the 2026 season, reaching 11.2 million. Watch time climbed nearly 50 percent, while subscribers grew 17.9 percent. Those gains are part of a strong first half of the season for the NWSL . Attendance is up, the league s social following has expanded, and merchandise sales have increased. New clubs and a wider mix of broadcast and streaming options are also creating more ways for people to follow the league. So, who are these new fans, how are they discovering the NWSL, and what is turning their interest into a lasting connection with the league? Who is watching the NWSL? The NWSL's audience is broader than the young, predominantly female fan base often associated with women's sports. Nielsen estimated that the league had 39.3 million fans in the United States in 2025, up from 30.8 million in 2023. On ESPN , women accounted for nearly half of the NWSL audience for the second consecutive season, while viewership among women ages 18 to 34 increased 30 percent year over year. The league s average audience on ESPN Deportes also more than doubled from 2024, rising 109 percent. Women now account for nearly half of the league's audience on ESPN, while women ages 18 to 34 are among its fastest-growing viewer groups. Spanish-language viewership is also rising, introducing the league to more fans across the United States. That growing audience is increasingly visible

title: Sony releases one last trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
author: Jennifer Ouellette
body: The Odyssey might be cleaning up at the box office at the moment, but in two weeks another mega-blockbuster hits theaters: Spider-Man: Brand New Day , directed by Destin Daniel Cretton ( Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings ). Sony Pictures dropped one last trailer for this follow-up to 2021’s No Way Home, which is purportedly intended to launch a fresh trilogy of films with Tom Holland in the title role. No Way Home ended on a pretty bleak note , with Peter Parker (Holland) asking Doctor Strange (Benedict Cumberbatch) to erase him from everyone’s memory to protect the multiverse, including his girlfriend MJ (Zendaya). Per the official synopsis: It’s a Brand New Day for Peter Parker. Fighting crime full-time as Spider-Man in a world that doesn’t remember him—and the pressure of seeing his old friends move on without him—sparks a change in Peter he may not have the power to control. But that transformation might also be the only thing that can stop a shocking new threat to the city and those he loves–a powerful villain no one can even see. The world may have forgotten Peter Parker, but he hasn’t forgotten them. Holland is reprising his role as Peter Parker/Spider-Man, along with Zendaya as MJ and Jacob Batalon as Peter’s former bestie Ned Leeds. title: Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
author: Beth Mole
body: Federal officials on Monday reaffirmed that iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms still appears to be the source of Cyclospora, the microscopic parasite behind a multi-state surge in explosive diarrhea cases. The assertion was intended to clear up confusion that began swirling over the weekend around a false positive test from the Food and Drug Administration —which Taylor Farms celebrated , sparking confusion. Still, many details of the outbreak remain murky, largely due to the vague recall information Taylor Farms has publicly provided. On Friday, the FDA, along with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, announced that the FDA's traceback investigations for the outbreak converged on Taylor Farms. Traceback investigations work by tracking backward from sick people and the foods they ate to where those foods came from through the supply chain, down to individual farms or production facilities. When the FDA said their investigation converged on Taylor Farms, the agency means that, over and over again, tracebacks led them to shredded iceberg lettuce grown in Central Mexico and supplied by Taylor Farms—which also goes by Taylor Fresh Foods. Read full article Comments

title: Nintendo customers have no legal right to tariff refunds, company tells judge
author: Jon Brodkin
body: Nintendo urged a court to dismiss a lawsuit demanding that it pass tariff refunds on to customers, saying that people who bought a Switch console or other products have no legal right to refunds that Nintendo is due to receive from the government. Nintendo said in a motion to dismiss yesterday that plaintiffs are "ask[ing] this Court to invent a legal duty out of whole cloth to retroactively re-price completed sales simply because the legal landscape has changed." Nintendo said the plaintiffs "have no legal entitlement to the tariff refunds Nintendo stands to receive." The lawsuit was filed in April by two customers who seek to represent a class of all US residents who bought Nintendo products from February 2025 to February 2026. California resident Gregory Hoffert and Washington resident Prashant Sharan filed the class action complaint in US District Court for the Western District of Washington, alleging unjust enrichment and a violation of the Washington Consumer Protection Act's prohibition on unfair or deceptive acts. title: Let Tom Hiddleston be your guide to Pompeii's final day
author: Jennifer Ouellette
body: When Mount Vesuvius erupted in 79 CE, it spewed molten rock, pumice, and hot ash over the cities of Pompeii and Herculaneum , killing thousands of people. It's one of the most famous natural disasters in human history, so naturally there have been countless documentaries about Pompeii (and at least one popular song ). But only one features actor Tom Hiddleston taking on the role of a "time detective" to bring the people of Pompeii to vivid life. That would be National Geographic's new three-part docuseries, Pompeii: Out of Time . The Marvel connection made Out of Time happen. Executive producer Kevin Wright was also a producer on the Disney+ series Loki , which included a scene where Hiddleston's Loki travels through time to visit Pompeii. Hiddleston also studied classics at Cambridge University and had visited the Pompeii archaeological site as a teenager, which the Marvel star attributes to indirectly influencing his career path into acting. "There is just a passion to the way Tom tells stories that's infectious," Wright told Ars. "We knew that if we could capture that and put it into [the series], it might interest people who wouldn't normally want to watch a documentary about Pompeii. " Read full article Comments

title: Love Island stars cant accept every post-villa offer. Heres why.
body: Leaving Love Island USA can give a contestant the kind of audience most aspiring creators spend years trying to build. By the time Islanders get their phones back, they may have millions of new followers, brands filling their inboxes, and television producers considering where they could appear next. But leaving the villa does not necessarily mean they are free to accept every opportunity waiting for them. That could matter for Mackenzie "Kenzie" Annis, whose commitment to practicing her splits throughout Season 8 inspired fans to campaign for her to join Dancing With the Stars . Professional dancer Ezra Sosa encouraged the idea beneath Annis' first post-villa TikTok. "The mirrorball is CALLING your name," Sosa commented. "SHUT THE FRONT DOOR," Annis replied. Turning that exchange into an actual trip to the ballroom may require more than an invitation from ABC. According to The Hollywood Reporter , Love Island USA contestants enter a one-year exclusivity period that can limit their television appearances, brand partnerships, and other commercial work after the show. Because Dancing With the Stars airs on ABC and Disney+ , outside Peacock s NBCUniversal family, Annis may need approval before she can compete. Her situation provides a useful look at what happens after Islanders leave Fiji, when sudden internet fame collides with the contracts they signed before anyone knew their names. What happens after Islanders leave the villa? Love Island USA places a group of singles in a Fiji villa, where they date, compete in challenges, and try to remain coupled up long enough to win.

title: Naked mole-rat queens use a chemical signal to suppress fertility in rivals
author: Jacek Krywko
body: Naked mole rats are weird. They spend their lives almost entirely underground, are largely insensitive to some kinds of pain, can persist without oxygen for a long time, rarely develop cancer, and can live past 30 years, which is decades longer than similarly sized rodents. They are also eusocial, like ants, bees, or termites—in a colony that can hold more than a hundred animals, only one female, the queen, breeds. We didn’t know how exactly naked mole-rat queens stop other females from breeding. For a long time, our leading hypothesis was bullying and violence, but that may be a bit impractical, given their kingdoms are vast networks of tunnels that can stretch for up to three kilometers. But now a team of Lewin Lab scientists at Max Delbrück Center for Molecular Medicine in Berlin discovered it’s actually just one part of a sophisticated olfactory signaling program. Running on smell "It's very clear that mole rats actually have quite big noses and smell a lot," says Gary Lewin, a neurobiologist at the Max Delbrück Center for Molecular Medicine and senior author of the study. Naked mole rats have around 1200 olfactory receptor genes, more than mice (which have roughly 1,000 of them) and way more than the few hundred in humans. They're also blind. "They have to be able to navigate and move around without vision, so smell is one of those things that’s enhanced," Lewin said. Read full article Comments

title: X finally ships its rebuilt Android app, but dont expect big changes
body: X has officially rolled out a rebuilt version of its Android app , following through on a project the company first teased roughly a year ago. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. In an August 2025 post on X , Head of Product Nikita Bier announced he was recruiting what he called the "Android Dream Team," describing the effort in characteristically hyperbolic terms and asking engineers to help "rebuild the X Android app" from the ground up. The recruitment push came as users had periodically flagged bugs and performance issues with the app in the months since; for instance, tapping tweet links from other apps , like WhatsApp, would incorrectly load the main timeline instead of the intended post. Nearly a year after Bier's initial call for engineers, Bier announced on July 20 that the rebuild was complete, calling it one of the largest engineering projects in the company's history. He described the new app as faster, smoother, and more reliable, and said the updated foundation would let the team ship new features more quickly going forward. An X spokesperson elaborated on the changes in an email statement to Mashable: "X modernized the foundation so everything is faster, smoother, and more reliable, including scrolling, loading, and notifications." Beyond the underlying performance improvements, the update doesn't appear to introduce new user-facing features at launch. The company is framing this release as an infrastructure overhaul, with the spokesperson reiterating that Bier's posts on X, including support for hosting Spaces and be

body: Google has introduced two additions to its Gemini "Flash" lineup , along with a specialized cybersecurity model, 3.5 Flash Cyber, while holding off on releasing a long-awaited Gemini 3.5 Pro . In a July 21 blog post , Google announced Gemini 3.6 Flash and Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite, describing them as built to give developers the "efficiency, low latency, and reliability" needed to run AI agents at scale. The post also detailed Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized model paired with Google's CodeMender security agent. Our big Guessing Game is back! Enter now for a chance to win an Apple Watch . The announcement also included a brief update on Gemini 3.5 Pro , which Google says "is currently testing with partners," with general availability "as soon as it's ready." The post added that Google has already begun training runs for a future Gemini 4 model. At the Google I/O event in May, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai announced that Gemini 3.5 Pro would launch in June . Since then, Anthropic and OpenAI have launched new frontier models, Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol , while the Chinese AI lab Moonshot recently launched Kimi K3, a more affordable but still competitive open-source AI model. It's against this backdrop that Google announced its new trio of Gemini models. SEE ALSO: Where is Gemini 3.5 Pro? The AI model announced at Google I/O is still MIA. According to the post, Gemini 3.6 Flash is positioned as the company's general-purpose "workhorse" model, offering improvements in coding, knowledge work, and multimodal tasks compared to 3.5 Flash. title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out
author: Ashley Belanger
body: On Monday, a judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement between Anthropic and authors, ending the largest copyright class-action ever certified and granting the largest copyright settlement ever reached . Back in May, some authors fought to block the settlement , which was proposed after the court ruled that Anthropic training AI on books was fair use; however, its piracy of works was likely not. Authors opposing the settlement argued that lawyers’ fees were too high and authors’ payouts were too low. Hoping to avoid accepting the estimated $3,000-per-work payout and file separate lawsuits to seek higher damages, a handful of authors tried to opt out past the deadline. Read full article Comments

title: Sales of Valve's Steam Deck crater after recent price hike
author: Kyle Orland
body: Since its 2022 launch, the Steam Deck has consistently been at or near the top of the company's "top selling products by revenue" charts , with brief dips outside of the top 10 treated as newsworthy events . But sales of the popular handheld gaming PC have apparently been on a steep decline since the system relaunched at a higher price in May . Linux-focused gaming site Boiling Steam breaks down the historical data , showing how the Deck's chart position has fallen from fifth place immediately after orders resumed in late May down to 14th place for two weeks in early July (the hardware sits at 12th place in the current edition of those charts ). That's in sharp contrast to 2025, when the Steam Deck never dropped below seventh place on the bestseller charts and was only rarely outside the Top 5. Then Valve warned of "intermittent shortages" for the Steam Deck starting in February, leading to weeks of lower chart positions before the Deck became completely unavailable until May. Read full article Comments

title: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
author: Ryan Whitwam
body: Google announced a significant evolution of its AI models at I/O in May with the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash, and it's not slowing down. The company revealed three new AI models today , including its first version of Gemini geared toward cybersecurity. However, none of the new models is the delayed Gemini 3.5 Pro, which was supposed to launch in June. Gemini 3.5 Flash, which was the star of the show at I/O , has already been deprecated. In its place, developers and users will find Gemini 3.6 Flash. Google makes the usual claims about this model—it's marginally more capable and better at coding, and it has great multimodal features. Google says the changes to 3.6 Flash were made in response to user feedback on the 3.5 release. In general, Gemini 3.5 Flash didn't appear to live up to Google's promises around code generation. Perhaps that is simply a consequence of Google's intense focus on efficiency as businesses have started to fret over the cost of AI tokens. Read full article Comments

title: Pornhub traffic tanked in Spain and Argentina during World Cup final
body: Spain has triumphed as the winner of the 2026 FIFA World Cup , but not before an intense final and the first-ever World Cup halftime show . And, as usual, Pornhub is a barometer for what captured people's attention so much that they clicked away from explicit content. Many Spaniards and Argentines X'ed out of their Pornhub tabs in droves to focus on the game. At kickoff, Pornhub traffic was down 63 percent in Spain and a whopping 77 percent in Argentina, compared to an average Sunday on the tube site. By 5 p.m. ET, around two hours into the game, traffic was down 77 percent in Spain and 74 percent in Argentina (those missing three percent of Argentines had a feeling the final was over prematurely, apparently). Credit: Pornhub There were traffic dips worldwide, but nothing like those in Spain and Argentina. At kickoff, worldwide traffic was down 23 percent compared to an average Sunday, and 29 percent two hours into the game. In the U.S., people seemed to care even less about the gooooooal , with traffic just down 16 percent at kickoff and 21 percent at 5 p.m. ET. Credit: Pornhub Unlike Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show , which got people off the tube site, the World Cup halftime show didn't seem to cause a similar shift. Worldwide traffic was around 20 percent below average at the start, and it nearly returned to typical viewership during the show. Guess Ted Lasso wasn't enticing enough. Credit: Pornhub In the hours after the game ended, Pornhub traffic shot up way above average in Spain (+36 percent at around 9 p.m.), Argentina (+17 percent), and worldwide (+9 percent).

title: The Dink review: Apples pickleball movie is a laugh-out-loud crowd-pleaser
body: With the rise in popularity of pickleball, a "pickleball movie" was inevitable. But I'm frankly relieved that The Dink is in the hands of director Josh Greenbaum. He's the helmer of the brilliant buddy comedy Barb and Star Go to Vista del Mar and the buddy documentary Will Harper . Not only are both of these movies top-tier when it comes to feel-good films, but each centers on older women without making them the butt of any jokes about aging. Instead, both celebrate their heroines for their brightness, humor, horniness, and brave honesty. And The Dink follows in their footsteps, while being a bit more mainstream. The Dink brings together Jake Johnson, Mary Steenburgen, Ed Harris, Patton Oswalt, and Aaron Chen for a buddy comedy that's laugh-out-loud hilarious and terrifically heartwarming. But of course, it begins with the fiery rivalry between tennis players and pickleballers. The Dink is a sensationally silly sports comedy. Jake Johnson stars as lovable loser Dusty Boyd, who'll tell anyone who'll listen about his brush with long-passed glory. As a child, Dusty was a tennis prodigy, coached by his father Chuck (a comically mirthless Ed Harris) to be the next hard-hitting legend. But after a disastrous match against Andy Roddick (yes, Andy Roddick), his reputation was destroyed. So here he is, a lollygagging tennis coach to children at a club run by his father. But Dusty has a chance to redeem himself in his father's eyes when a new enemy invades their pristine tennis courts. You guessed it: pickleballers. In a deranged slideshow presentation, Chuck explains to Dusty how th

After skyrocketing to a high of $225 just days after the company's blockbuster IPO, SpaceX stock has been in what seems like a never-ending slide. SpaceX stock has now fallen for seven days in a row, and is now trading at a new low of $120. That's well below the $150 price point at which the SpaceX stock debuted. The stock's lackluster performance has resulted in SpaceX losing $1 trillion of its market value. In addition, Musk himself has lost his trillionaire status , a designation he was able to claim for a short time last month. How did we get here? Our big Guessing Game is back! Enter now for a chance to win an Apple Watch . SpaceX stock began to crater shortly after the company announced the acquisition of AI coding agent Cursor for $60 billion. SpaceX also recently received the lowest possible environmental, social, and governance (ESG) rating, a Triple-C ESG rating, from index provider MSCI. ESG is often utilized by investors and funds when determining the most ethical, least problematic stocks for investors, helping them avoid problematic companies such as arms manufacturers or polluters. On top of that, many investors have begun to get cold feet when it comes to AI investments, beyond just SpaceX. SpaceX, of course, isn't just a space exploration company anymore. title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe
author: Scharon Harding
body: The company behind the disk space analyzer TreeSize has irked some users by no longer offering support or updates for perpetual licenses beyond their maintenance period unless customers subscribe. Further frustration has come from JAM Software's long-standing policy of not providing license keys or installers to TreeSize perpetual license holders after that support period ends. Since 2025, JAM Software has been transitioning most TreeSize editions to subscription models. Today, it sells perpetual licenses only for personal use, which include 12 months of updates, support, and “downloads of older versions, and your license,” plus the option to extend the support period. TreeSize currently has "no plans to discontinue the sale of perpetual licenses for TreeSize Personal," product manager Hendrik Christ told Ars Technica. As perpetual-license maintenance periods expire, customers are discovering that extending support now generally requires subscribing to software they already own the right to use. title: The Light Flip is a minimalist flip phone with a point to prove
body: It’s a Light Phone. It’s a flip phone. | Photo: Light A few years ago, the team at Light shipped the most smartphone-like device they'd ever created. The Light Phone III still kept to its minimalist, no-distractions ethos, but it was a phone-size rectangle with a phone-size touchscreen, a camera, and a number of other features. Some people loved having an almost-smartphone, but Kaiwei Tang, Light's cofounder, says he started getting feedback he didn't expect. Lots of users, particularly young ones, didn't want a smartphone. More than that, actually: They didn't even want something that looked like a smartphone. "We have users who tell us, 'Hey, I hate smartphones. I don't want to be seen using … Read the full story at The Verge.

The cybersecurity model is built upon Gemini 3.5 Flash and will be available first to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender, Google's security-focused coding agent . As noted by Google, CodeMender can call upon 3.5 Flash Cyber "multiple times at high speed and low cost," allowing the AI agents to … Read the full story at The Verge.
