title: Light Flip is a minimalistic, cheap flip phone that wont distract you
body: Remember Light , the New York-based company that builds super-simple , distraction-free, cheap smartphones ? It's back with a new device; its first-ever flip phone called the Light Flip. SEE ALSO: Galaxy Z Flip 8 rumors: Release date, colors, specs, and more Once again, this is a cheap phone designed to free you from endless scrolling and social media addiction , while still offering the basic tools you need for everyday life. To do this, the company has built an Android-based, minimalistic operating system called LightOS that's mostly textual and displays everything in just two colors. You do get access to music, messages, alarms, and other basic apps, but you don't get to scroll the flashy new TIkTok video; that's what your other phone is for. The Light Flip is available in six colors: Black, Navy, Red, Pink, Yellow, and Light Gray. Credit: Light Phone The Light Flip doesn't stray far from the formula, but approaches it with the body of a flip phone. In many ways, it's far more simple than the company's previous phone, the Light Phone III, which has an OLED touchscreen. The Light Flip is basically your flip phone of old, with a plastic body, a numeric, T9 keypad, some function buttons, and a 2.8-inch, non-touch display. Numeric keypad? That's right, but at least it supports T9 predictive input. Credit: Light Phone Inside, it's powered by a MediaTek MT8873 chip, 6GB of RAM, 128GB of storage, and has a 50-megapixel camera and a (replaceable) 1,800mAh battery. Other amenities include a headphone jack (nice!), a USB-C port, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and 5G connectivity. The camera on

title: Garmin just launched the screen-free Cirqa Smart Band, and it looks a lot like Whoop
body: Garmin just debuted its latest fitness tracker , the Cirqa Smart Band, and it looks a lot like a Whoop . Opens in a new window Credit: Garmin Garmin Cirqa Smart Band $199.99 Learn More When the Oura Ring launched, it unleashed a barrage of competitors coming out with their own smart rings . Now, Whoop is getting that same treatment. First, there was the Fitbit Air , and now, Garmin's Cirqa Smart Band is the latest screen-free health tracker to hit the market. The band is designed for round-the-clock wear, able to do so with 10 days of battery life. Data collected populates in the Garmin Connect app, where users access all their biometrics, including heart rate, Body Battery energy monitoring, Pulse Ox, stress, and skin temperature. Similar to Whoop and Oura, it provides insights into women's health and cycles, compatible with the Natural Cycles app. Even without a screen, the Cirqa detects and records activity. The fabric band is designed for all-day comfort, coming in citron gray, mauve, French gray, dark olive, captain blue, French blue, and black. Plus, it's designed to be worn around the wrist or as an arm band. It comes in two sizes, small/medium and large/extra-large. But unlike the OG screen-free tracker, Whoop, the Cirqa is subscription-free. That means all you have to do is buy the band to gain access to the full suite of features. The Circa Smart Band will be available to order on July 24 for $199.99.

title: Other Mommy trailer: Yes, the one traumatizing moviegoers ahead of The Odyssey
body: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey has some truly spooky moments. (Team Circe.) But fans who took to theaters this opening weekend were shook by a surprising trailer ahead of the big show. Meet Other Mommy. If you screamed at that jump scare, you're not alone. Jessica Chastain, who's previously starred in such horror gems as Momma and IT: Chapter Two , returns to the genre with a twisted double-turn. Based on the New York Times bestselling novel Incidents Around the House by Josh Malerman, Other Mommy centers on a little girl named Bela (Arabella Olivia Clark), who's formed a disturbing bond with an entity that looks like her mother. Chastain plays both roles, while Jay Duplass lends support as Daddo. Beyond this promising trailer and an enticing adult cast, Other Mommy also boasts intriguing talent behind the scenes. Horror icon James Wan produces for Blumhouse Atomic Monster, while director Rob Savage ( Host , Dashcam , The Boogeyman ) takes the helm. From the looks of this trailer, he's primed another scream-rich stunner sure to thrill audiences beyond this impactful trailer. Other Mommy opens in theaters Oct. 9.

title: A touchscreen and light make the new X4 Pro the best version of Xteink’s tiny e-readers
body: If you’ve been undecided on which Xteink device to get, the new X4 Pro is it. The familiar story with Xteink’s tiny e-readers plays out once again with its new X4 Pro: upgraded hardware held back by frustrating software. The company’s latest tiny e-reader adds a touchscreen and lighting, so choosing it over a Kindle or a Kobo feels like less of a compromise — until you use it. The new touch controls and some welcome software updates do manage to make the X4 Pro easier to use, but frustrations still abound. There’s a happier ending to the story this time, however, as the team behind the excellent CrossPoint Reader software plan to have their alternative firmware ready for the new device at its launch. If you’ve been considering one of Xteink’s e-readers, the X4 Pro is the one to get, even if it’s the most expensive yet. The X4 Pro is available for purchase today through Xteink’s online store for $99, making it slightly more expensive than the company’s $79 X3 and $69 X4 models . That also brings the X4 Pro closer in price to the $110 entry-level Kindle featuring Amazon’s polished e-reader experience and on-device access to a well-stocked digital bookstore. The X4 Pro lacks both of those things, but with a 4.26-inch, 800 x 480 E Ink screen, it’s another extremely pocketable device from Xteink that’s slightly smaller and thinner than the company’s first e-reader, and considerably smaller than Amazon’s 6-inch Kindle. That thin profile means you’ll need to be prepared for a big compromise: There’s no longer an actual USB-C port. You can still benefit from USB-C functionality but

title: Xteink is back with a new generation of its tiny X4 e-reader — but it wont be for everyone
body: Xteink is back with the second generation of its X4 e-reader. The X4 Pro is here and redesigned, but are there improvements? Xteink's tiny e-readers, which are bigger than a credit card but smaller than a passport, get plenty of attention for their small size, which supposedly can fit on the back of a phone. The X4 has previously made our list of the best e-readers as the best mini e-reader, so we had to try out the second generation ourselves About the Xteink X4 Pro A little bigger than a credit card, the Xteink X4 Pro is perfectly pocket-sized. Credit: Samantha Mangino / Mashable The Xteink X4 Pro, the second generation of the X4, comes with a 4.26-inch display and 219 ppi resolution (Kindle and Kobo both have 300 ppi). It has 8MB RAM and 16MB of storage with up to 256GB of expandable space with a microSD card. It has a 10+ hour battery life, which is about one to two weeks of casual reading, and has a touchscreen with physical buttons on the side. Plus, there's a circular home button, reminiscent of the old iPhone button. The touchscreen is new to the X4, which also has a frontlight. It has a magnetic back, which is compatible with MagSafe, so it can snap to the back of an iPhone. Rather than a USB-C port, it also comes with a magnetic charging port, which uses USB-C. The Xteink X4 Pro charges through a magnetic pogo pin. Credit: Samantha Mangino / Mashable It connects to WiFi, and books are imported to the e-reader via the Xteink app, USB-C, or microSD card. It only supports DRM-free books in text-only PDF and MOBI format, in addition to JPG and BMP image files. The new

title: Spain’s World Cup champions celebrated with custom Oakley Meta glasses
body: TL;DR: Spain s World Cup winners celebrated their victory wearing a one-of-one pair of custom Oakley Meta glasses created exclusively for the team. While fans can t buy that exact edition, Oakley s AI glasses show how wearable tech could help athletes capture plays, review performance, and train smarter. Opens in a new window Credit: Meta Meta Oakley Vanguard Smart AI Glasses $499 at Amazon Shop Now Spain s World Cup celebration came with a special piece of tech. After winning the championship, the Spanish team celebrated wearing custom Oakley Meta glasses made just for the players. The one-of-one edition features custom packaging and lenses etched with the FIFA World Cup Champions mark. While fans can t buy this exact pair, you can get the same AI-powered experience with the Oakley Meta glasses . Credit: FIFA Oakley Meta Vanguard is designed around the needs of athletes. The glasses can capture hands-free footage, letting players record practices, game highlights, or specific plays simply by saying, Hey Meta, take a video. The built-in 12MP ultra-wide camera records in 3K, while stabilization helps keep footage smoother during quick movements like sprints, cuts, and tackles. The glasses can also help players review their performance after the game. When connected to apps like Strava or compatible devices like Garmin , Oakley Meta Vanguard can overlay performance data like sprint speed, distance, and heart rate on captured content, giving athletes more context around their training sessions. title: When your vehicle outlives its cloud: What happens next?
author: Michael Harley
body: Do you use your vehicle’s app to pre‑cool the cabin on a scorching day, confirm the doors are locked from bed, or have the car automatically call for help when the airbags go off? Savor those connected, cloud‑based conveniences while you can—the clock is already ticking on their obsolescence. In a few short years, you may be driving today’s vehicle without many of the features you currently enjoy. How we got hooked on the cloud "Connected services"—cloud-based and networked features that let a vehicle communicate with external systems to deliver apps, data, and remote functions to drivers and passengers—have been around since the 1990s, when GM introduced OnStar as an option. Those early systems were mostly voice‑centric, but they still depended on centralized back‑end systems. Modern versions, which are fully data-driven and cloud-hosted, use much more sophisticated over-the-air data links to allow a considerably better feature set. Read full article Comments

title: Apps targeted at US troops contain Chinese and Russian code
author: Dell Cameron, wired.com
body: A recent examination of hundreds of mobile apps marketed toward US military personnel found more than one in eight contained software built by companies in China , Russia , or other foreign nations, raising fresh concerns that adversary governments could harvest data revealing where service members live, work, and deploy. According to researchers at Purdue University, the US Military Academy at West Point, and Florida International University , one popular app used by service members to rate living conditions on their own bases include code from Huawei, the Chinese telecom that US regulators flagged as a national security threat in 2020. Two others were built by Russian companies and incorporate the Russian ad service Yandex. The largely unregulated advertising industry that tracks Americans online treats civilians and service members mostly the same—unless there is profit in telling them apart —despite evidence that exposure can reveal troop deployments, unit movements, and the routines of personnel within intelligence facilities and hardened shelters where nuclear weapons are believed to be stored. title: 2027 Kia Seltos first drive gives you plenty of SUV for not much $
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Kia provided flights from Washington, DC, to Orange County, California, and accommodation so Ars could drive the Seltos. Ars does not accept paid editorial content. NEWPORT BEACH, Calif.—Although it sometimes feels like automakers have all but abandoned everything other than high-end luxury model vehicles, it's not universally true. While some car companies have lobbied the government, terrified, asking to build protective walls to fend off cheap Chinese competition, others have looked at the problem from a different angle, trying to meet the moment. One such effort is the new Kia Seltos, a small SUV that takes inspiration from the Telluride but with a starting price of just $24,990 plus delivery charge. The US almost didn't get the Seltos in the first place; the first-generation model was designed with China in mind. Seltos take two is more considered, and also a little more substantial. The new body with its Telluride-like design cues has been stretched, mostly in the wheelbase. That said, at 174.4 inches (4,430 mm) long, 72 inches (1,829 mm) wide, and 64.9 inches (1,648 mm) tall, it's still a subcompact, one segment smaller than something like a Toyota RAV4 or Mazda CX-5 . Kia is offering a choice of three different powertrain variants. The 1.6 L hybrid is the one we're most interested in. But that version won't be in showrooms until Q4 this year; don't expect an Ars first drive of the hybrid Seltos until early next year. Or any other details in this first drive report—the hybrid Seltos is still undergoing its EPA certification, and Kia isn't prepared to talk power, torq

title: Nintendo keeps making shooters more friendly and approachable
body: Nintendo does a lot of weird things , but Splatoon might just be one of the strangest - or at least the boldest. The series started as an attempt to muscle into the ultra-competitive online shooter space, but in a way that feels a world away from the violence and bombast of Call of Duty or Battlefield . Splatoon is essentially candy-coated paintball starring a race of skate punk squid creatures , and across three entries Nintendo has just about perfected the formula. The series introduced the idea of competitive shooters to whole new audiences, and now it's expanding: Splatoon Raiders takes it in a different direction, but with the same focus o … Read the full story at The Verge.

body: I was not a fan of the original Halliday glasses. The Gen 2 are better. | Image: Halliday I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. It was an interesting concept with terrible execution, especially compared to the several other smart glasses on the show floor . So I was skeptical when a few weeks ago, Halliday reached out to say its second attempt at smart glasses was much better. Well, having tried the Halliday Gen 2, I'm happy to report these are better glasses. That secret display window is gone, replaced by more traditional but dis … Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Splatoon Raiders is a spiffy single-player makeover for Nintendos coolest series
body: I love Splatoon . I have loved Splatoon since all the way back to its Wii U debut in 2015. But Splatoon doesn't always love me back. Every time I boot up Splatoon 3 , I rediscover I'm not ready for the series' signature competitive multiplayer arenas. Everyone who still plays Splatoon this long after launch is really, really good too good for me to have much of a good time. The smattering of solo and co-op experiences that exist on the periphery can't hold my attention for very long. It is delightful, then, to report that Splatoon Raiders is pretty damn good. The first single-player-focused entry in the decade-old series is one of the most purely fun (on a moment-to-moment basis) games on the Nintendo Switch 2 . Splatoon Raiders synthesizes some of the best side modes in previous Splatoon games into something that grabbed me and didn't let go for several days, thanks in part to some non-competitive co-op features. I can't wait to play more once it goes on sale. SEE ALSO: Nintendo's potentially last Switch 1 game is absolutely worth your time Splatoon Raiders melds every non-competitive Splatoon mode into one game I say this not as criticism, but as a compliment: Splatoon Raiders feels like DLC for a mainline Splatoon game that grew out of control. This is still a third-person shooter where you, as a squid kid, use a variety of weapons to spread ink of your own color across a variety of battlefields. You don't run very fast, but if your ink color is on the ground, you can quickly swim around in it, recovering ammo and health in the process. Everything about the basic mechani

title: Which countries have banned social media for kids?
body: Social media bans for kids are quickly becoming the global legislation trend of the moment. Children under 16 are finding themselves at the center of political conversations, with countries including Australia , the UK , Canada , Brazil , Austria , and Indonesia , and more attempting to wrangle Big Tech companies' hold on young people's attention. SEE ALSO: Australia's kid social media ban is ineffective, study suggests Online age verification laws are taking shape by the month, with governments citing the need to address issues including child sexual exploitation , cyberbullying , self-harm , and the impact on kids' mental health , as well as "addictive" design, violent content , and porn . However, these laws have also come under severe criticism for efficiency and their impact on digital privacy and free speech . Plus, kids are already finding ways around them. So, where are we at, globally? Which countries have followed Australia's example and are rolling out their own social media bans? Here's the list, alphabetically, and incidentally starting with the one who started it all. Australia The first to do it, Australia set a global precedent in Dec. 2025 with its world-first social media ban for children under 16 . The Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act requires social media platforms such as TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, X, and Snapchat to implement age verification and take "reasonable steps" to prevent kids under 16 from creating accounts. However, a July 2026 study by Australian government advisors found the ban so far ineffective . Plus, R

title: Magic: The Gatherings Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Team-Up Box is down to its best-ever price at Amazon — save $20
body: TL;DR: The Magic: The Gathering Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Team-Up Box is on sale for $29.99 at Amazon , down from its $49.99 list price. Opens in a new window Credit: Magic: The Gathering Magic: The Gathering Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Team-Up $29.99 at Amazon $49.99 Save $20.00 Get Deal Magic: The Gathering s TMNT crossover has plenty of products built around opening packs, but Turtle Team-Up makes it so you can have even more trading card fun with your friends. And now Amazon has made that co-operative box cheaper than we ve ever seen. As of July 21, the Magic: The Gathering Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Team-Up Box is available at Amazon for $29.99 cutting $20 from its $49.99 list price, giving you roughly 40% off the complete multiplayer set. This not only dips well under TCGplayer s market price of $36.80 , but is also the lowest price we ve ever seen the TMNT-themed MTG box on Amazon, as confirmed with price tracker camelcamelcamel . To be clear, this is not another standard booster box designed for opening packs. Turtle Team-Up is designed for two to four players working together against an automated enemy system, rather than trying to knock one another out. Games are expected to last around 45 minutes, making it an easy fit for a casual Magic night without turning the whole evening into one drawn-out match. Mashable Deals Be the first to know! title: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 21, 2026
body: Today's Wordle answer should be easy to solve if you can keep a secret. If you just want to be told today's word, you can jump to the bottom of this article for today's Wordle solution revealed. But if you'd rather solve it yourself, keep reading for some clues, tips, and strategies to assist you. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 21, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive? The entire archive of past Wordle puzzles was originally available for anyone to enjoy whenever they felt like it, but it was later taken down , with the website's creator stating it

title: NYT Strands hints, answers for July 21, 2026
body: Today's NYT Strands hints are easy if you're a good writer. Strands , the New York Times ' elevated word-search game, requires the player to perform a twist on the classic word search. Words can be made from linked letters up, down, left, right, or diagonal, but words can also change direction, resulting in quirky shapes and patterns. Every single letter in the grid will be part of an answer. There's always a theme linking every solution, along with the "spangram," a special, word or phrase that sums up that day's theme, and spans the entire grid horizontally or vertically. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable By providing an opaque hint and not providing the word list, Strands creates a brain-teasing game that takes a little longer to play than its other games, like Wordle and Connections . If you're feeling stuck or just don't have 10 or more minutes to figure out today's puzzle, we've got all the NYT Strands hints for today's puzzle you need to progress at your preferred pace. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 21, 2026 NYT Strands hint for today s theme: Write there The words are related to paper. Today s NYT Strands theme plainly explained These words describe writing utensils. NYT Strands spangram hint: Is it vertical or horizontal? Today's NYT Strands spangram is horizontal. Meet The Mashable 101: Our list of the content creators shaping the internet today NYT Strands spangram answer today Today's spangram is Stationery. title: macOS 27 Beta has a secret Siri AI interface. How to try it.
body: You've waited long enough for Apple's relaunch of Siri as an AI assistant, and now you can finally try out the Siri AI beta for iOS . But, psst, don't tell anyone, but there's even more Siri AI for you to try out with a secret Siri AI interface on Mac. A Reddit user on the r/MacOSBeta subreddit has discovered a "hidden lightweight UI" for Siri AI in the macOS 27 Golden Gate beta . This feature basically provides users with an array of AI tools, presented in a pop-up menu whenever text is highlighted. Reddit The Siri AI pop-up interface includes AI tools such as Rewrite, Proofread, "How does this sound?," and "Edit with Siri." There are also menu options for summarization and providing bullet points from the selected text. Siri AI can also provide a selection of specific tools dependent on the context of what's selected. For example, highlighting a phone number provides a pop-up to "Add to contacts" or when selecting an address the pop-up offers an "Open in Maps" option. There are tools for creating calendar events, composing messages and emails, and tracking flights and packages as well. Why is the feature hidden? Well, as MacRumors points out, the Siri AI interface in macOS 27 developer beta 3 and public beta 1 is simply an unfinished product. Certain features don't yet work how they're supposed to. Some options don't work at all. Clearly, Apple has yet to make it available because it's simply not ready. However, Reddit users say there is a way to turn the interface on in the macOS Golden Gate Beta. According to Redditor pdfu, if users running the macOS 27 beta simply need

title: DA: Cop covered bodycam to snap nude prisoners on his iPhone—but other cams caught him
author: Nate Anderson
body: On March 31, 2026, a rather odd complaint arrived at the Bucks County, Pennsylvania District Attorney's Office. It came from the local sheriff's office, and it concerned one of their own, Deputy Sheriff Ryan Gaffney. The allegation was unusual: Gaffney had used his iPhone to snap photos of nude prisoners he had encountered in his job—and he had then shared those photos with "several female civilian employees inside the Sheriff's Office." The main claim involved a local man suffering a mental health crisis on the morning of January 30, 2026. Five fully uniformed deputies had arrived at the house and were in the upstairs bedroom. Their detainee was naked from the waist down and had just been persuaded to put on some pants. Read full article Comments

title: AI just disproved a major math problem. A mathematician explains.
body: While the rest of the world was watching the World Cup on Sunday evening, mathematician Levent Alp ge casually announced on X that he had used Anthropic's Fable 5 to disprove the Jacobian conjecture. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. The Jacobian conjecture is a long-standing open problem in algebraic geometry that's bedeviled highly accomplished mathematicians for almost 90 years. It was included in "Smale's problems," a list of unresolved math problems put forward by mathematician Stephen Smale in 1998. But that proved no obstacle for Fable 5, Anthropic's latest frontier AI model. (You may remember Fable 5 as the public version of Claude Mythos Preview , the AI model that Anthropic says has such advanced cybersecurity capabilities that it was too dangerous for public release .) Alp ge is part of Harvard's Society of Fellows, and his Linkedin profile notes an affiliation with Anthropic. We've reached out to Anthropic with questions about Alp ge's work. Meanwhile, for those of us who don't understand polynomial functions from n-dimensional space, the question is: Does this represent a major breakthrough in artificial intelligence, or mathematics, or neither? "This did not cause me to update my priors about what AI can and can't do," professor and mathematician Andrew Blumberg told Mashable. "This is exactly the kind of thing I would expect AI to be able to do. If there was a counterexample that was concise and easy to state that people haven't found because it's a pain to search through all this stuff, AI will find it." Blumberg,

title: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
author: Jeremy Hsu
body: Drones capable of spraying water and fire retardants have been practicing how to snuff out wildfires early in California and Alaska this summer—demonstrating a possible rapid-response tool for firefighting as climate change transforms wildfire season into a nearly year-round risk for much of the United States. Most drones are significantly smaller than crewed aircraft and have shorter flight ranges, meaning they cannot replace large airtankers in delivering massive payloads of water or other fire-suppressing payloads to remote wildfires. But companies and fire agencies—along with organizers of the $11 million XPRIZE competition—are testing whether drones can help firefighting crews respond more quickly to small fires and put them out before they become more destructive. The California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection, also known as CAL FIRE, ran its own field test involving five autonomous drones that worked together to deploy between 500 and 1,000 gallons of foam combined for suppressing fires on July 15, according to the TV station KPMH . The demonstration was organized with the help of the nonprofit FireWERX and the California-based company Seneca , which is making the drones commercially available starting in 2026. title: Judge halts Paramount's $111B purchase of Warner Bros. in win for US states
author: Jon Brodkin
body: A federal judge today ordered Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros. Discovery to halt their $111 billion merger, handing an early win to states that sued to block the deal. The ruling granted a temporary restraining order prohibiting the firms from completing the merger and from consolidating their operations. The temporary order is only in effect for 14 days, but it can be converted into a preliminary injunction that would prevent the merger from being completed until the case is resolved. The temporary restraining order can be extended past the 14-day period if more time is needed to rule on a preliminary injunction. A group of 12 states led by California sued the companies last week in an attempt to block the deal, which had been approved by the Trump administration . The states say the merger will eliminate competition by combining two of the five major Hollywood movie studios, and two of the five major owners of basic cable TV channels. title: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
author: Stephen Clark
body: It seems as though $5.6 billion wasn't enough. That was the news from the US Space Force on Friday, when military officials announced they were tripling the maximum value of one of the service's National Security Space Launch contracts to $17 billion. The expansion of the Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3 contract comes as the Pentagon signals rising demand for military satellite launches. The NSSL program is set up to allow Space Systems Command, which oversees the Space Force's launch program, to select from a pool of launch providers for individual missions to deliver the military's satellites to orbit. The NSSL program has two parts. Lane 1 covers the Space Force's more risk-tolerant missions, such as medium-lift launches with experimental payloads or rideshare missions carrying satellites for the Pentagon's surveillance or data relay constellations . Lane 2 includes higher-priority strategic missions, like the government's largest and most expensive spy satellites, or radiation-hardened communications satellites designed to survive a nuclear war. Read full article Comments

title: Get Borderlands 3, Risk of Rain 2 and 13 other great PC games for $15
body: Borderlands 3 is among the 15 games in the 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle. | 2K Games The aptly-named “2K Megahits 2026 Bundle” from Humble includes 15 Steam games for $15. It’s a smattering of the publisher 2K’s biggest games of the 2010s, including BioShock Infinite, Risk of Rain 2, Borderlands 3, and XCOM: Enemy Unknown . The bundle will be available until August 7th, though the bundle page notes that keys for some games are already starting to run low. All of the games work on Windows, while some support MacOS and SteamOS. 2K Megahits 2026 Bundle Where to Buy: $465 $15 at Humble One of my personal favorite games, Risk of Rain 2 , is included in the bundle. In the third-person roguelike, you’ll face off against increasingly difficult waves of enemies, fighting to stay alive as long as you can and collecting power-ups and useful perks before extracting to the next zone to do it all again. It’s just challenging enough to make victory satisfying, and fun to play alone or with friends. You also get 2012’s XCOM: Enemy Unknown , a legendary turn-based strategy game that’s usually $15 on its own, to give you a sense of the bundle’s value. The bundle also notably includes three games from the Borderlands universe. Borderlands 3 Special Edition is a traditional looter-shooter FPS, Tiny Tina’s Wonderlands adds RPG elements like an overworld map and spellcasting, and Tales from the Borderlands is an interactive episodic visual novel from Telltale Games. title: The moments that defined the 2026 World Cup
body: Every four years, the World Cup reminds us why we fall in love with sports in the first place. The 2026 tournament was one of firsts. For the first time, three countries Canada, Mexico, and the United States shared hosting duties. For the first time, the field expanded from 32 to 48 teams, giving more nations a chance to compete on the sport's biggest stage and more federations the opportunity to showcase their depth. That expansion brought historic debuts for Cape Verde, Jordan, Cura ao, and Uzbekistan. Their first appearances delivered some of the tournament's most unforgettable moments. It also felt like a farewell tour. Cristiano Ronaldo. Lionel Messi. Luka Modrić. Neymar. Son Heung-min. Guillermo Ochoa. A generation that shaped the sport for two decades took one last bow on the biggest stage, with several likely making their final World Cup appearances. Taken together, those storylines made this World Cup feel bigger than any single match. Over the course of several weeks, the tournament gave us unforgettable goals, shocking upsets, viral celebrations, heartbreaking exits, and moments that transcended soccer altogether. Here are the moments that defined the 2026 World Cup. Vozinha and the Blue Sharks The best feel-good story of the World Cup has to be Cape Verde's historic run and their goalkeeper, Vozinha (n e Josimar Jos vora Dias). title: AT&T loses key ruling in bid to stop offering basic phone service in California
author: Jon Brodkin
body: California can keep enforcing rules that require AT T to offer basic phone service to new customers in its wireline territory, following a federal judge's ruling last week. AT T sued California in May in a bid to end the state's Carrier of Last Resort (COLR) rules that require it to offer telephone service to any potential customer in its territory. AT T asked for a preliminary injunction that would prevent California from enforcing the COLR rules while the litigation continues. To win a preliminary injunction, AT T had to show it is likely to succeed on the merits of its claim that California rules are preempted by a Federal Communications Commission order. US District Judge Linda Lopez denied AT T's request for a preliminary injunction during a motion hearing on Thursday, according to a docket entry . The case is in US District Court for the Southern District of California. Read full article Comments

The first offering will be a $30,000 electric truck, engineered for efficiency and also — perhaps most importantly — profitability. The heavily camouflaged truck has already been spotted out in the wild, with observers noting that it seems even smaller than the Maverick. Success isn’t guaranteed. title: RIP bargain bin: The price impact of Sony's disc-free PlayStation plan
author: Kyle Orland
body: Since Sony announced that it was planning to stop producing physical PlayStation discs in 2028 , many people have been justifiably worried about how they will maintain ownership and long-term game access in a disc-free world. But halting production of PlayStation discs also means eliminating the market for cheap used PlayStation discs, which are often available for well below the price of a digital download. How much is this disc-based discount worth? To find out, Ars analyzed how the prices of 19 current top-selling PlayStation games varied between digital downloads and physical discs (both new and used). We found that, while used discs are the cheapest option for most games for most of the year, the frequent and periodic deep discounts offered on the PlayStation Store often undercut the cheapest game disc options. That state of affairs will likely persist even in a future without PlayStation discs. Our prices are so low, you'll think we've suffered brain damage For our analysis, we started with Sony's list of the bestsellers on the PlayStation Store . We eliminated any games that weren't available on a physical disc, then used the database over at PlatPrices.com to determine both the "Standard" price and the lowest "Discount" price offered for those games on the PlayStation Store in the last year (as well as the total number of days the discount price was offered). Read full article Comments

title: Kalshi gains 3 million users amid World Cup gambling
body: Spain has won the 2026 FIFA World Cup and millions of new Kalshi users either won or lost money betting on the game. According to prediction market Kalshi, the platform brought in 3 million new users during the World Cup tournament, which kicked off on June 11 and ended with the Spain vs. Argentina final on July 19. In addition to the millions of new users, the company said that the World Cup broke a platform record for the most money traded in a single market. Kalshi says more than $1.2 billion in trades, the term the company uses to describe bets, were made to predict the winner of the World Cup. Kalshi had a large presence throughout the World Cup. The company partnered with stars of the sport, such as Luka Modrić of the Croatia national team, who served as a brand ambassador and appeared in a commercial. Kalshi also had a partnership with the Argentina national team and received a featured post for the brand on Lionel Messi's Instagram account, which has over 512 million followers. A commercial featuring A-lister Timoth e Chalamet also appeared frequently in the U.S. market. Despite their constant presence throughout the tournament, neither Kalshi nor its largest competitor, Polymarket, were official sponsors of the FIFA World Cup. A much smaller, new prediction market called ADI Predictstreet was deemed the "Official Prediction Market Partner of the FIFA World Cup 2026." However, Kalshi even found a way in there. title: Jimothy the raccoon is taking over the internet
body: A very unusual raccoon from Seattle has become the internet's newest sweetheart. Since appearing in a brief video filmed in the city s Ballard neighborhood, Jimothy has accumulated millions of views and inspired murals, tattoos, merchandise, government public-service announcements, a city proclamation, and even a surprise appearance at a Seattle Mariners game. Online, fans have promoted him to "King Jimothy," "Saint Jimothy," and Seattle s "sweet prince." "Hot Jimothy Summer," a play on Megan Thee Stallion's "hot girl summer," has become the unofficial slogan surrounding the unusually shaped animal. SEE ALSO: The internet is worried about Punch the monkey. The zoo says he's OK. Jimothy s sudden fame began with one fairly reasonable question: What exactly are we looking at? This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. Meet Jimothy, Seattle's unusually shaped raccoon Kiana Hall encountered Jimothy while walking with her husband through Ballard on July 13. Hall initially thought the animal moving beneath a parked car was a cat and pulled out her phone to get a better look. Then it emerged with the familiar masked face of a raccoon and a much less familiar body shape. Hall posted the video on Instagram and named the raccoon Jimothy, a decision based less on a complicated backstory than an immediate feeling. "He just looked like a 'Jimothy' to me," Hall told TODAY.com . "I don't have any other explanation other than I looked at him and he looked like a 'Jimothy.'" The name stuck. The video garnered millions of views, while people around Ballard b

title: LG’s glossy OLED gaming monitor is rare to find under $400
body: LG’s UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor deliver deep, true-to-life blacks. | Image: LG If you’ve been thinking about upgrading your gaming monitor, LG’s 27-inch 27GX704A-B pairs a glossy WOLED panel with a fast refresh rate, and it’s currently on sale for $379.99 (about $70 off) at Amazon and directly from LG , marking a new low price. It originally launched at $799.99, but has been available for under $500 since May. LG UltraGear Glossy OLED gaming monitor (27-inches) Where to Buy: $449 $379.99 at Amazon $449 $379.99 at LG The monitor offers a 1440p resolution and a glossy coating, which isn’t a combination of specs we often see at this price. Most OLED monitors under $400 use QD-OLED panels, which can give blacks a slight bluish tint. LG’s WOLED monitor delivers truer-looking blacks while the glossy coating helps colors appear richer and more vibrant. It doesn’t sacrifice speed, either. The monitor boasts a fast 240Hz refresh rate with variable refresh rate support (Nvidia G-Sync compatible and AMD FreeSync Premium Pro), along with a fast 0.03ms response time to keep games looking sharp in motion. It comes with a solid array of ports for the price, with two HDMI 2.1 ports, DisplayPort 1.4, and USB-A ports, making it easy to connect a PC, consoles, and accessories. Rounding things out, the stand is fully adjustable, too, allowing you to raise, lower, tilt, swivel, or rotate the display into portrait mode for a more comfortable setup. Or, because it has a VESA mount, you can yank it off and toss it onto a monitor arm of your choice.

title: What does The Odyssey translator Emily Wilson think of Christopher Nolans film?
body: We can't ask Homer what he thinks of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey , but we can ask someone who knows it better than most. Emily Wilson, the British-American classicist whose 2017 translation of the epic poem was one of the director's inspirations , was asked her opinion on The Odyssey film by the Associated Press . "I want to celebrate the fact that so many more people are aware of The Odyssey and were brought back to the poem, she told AP. "But at the same time, I had the sense that Christopher Nolan was trying to pack everything in while missing themes that were essential to the poem." SEE ALSO: 'The Odyssey' hints at 3 myths without detailing them. Here are the untold stories. Nolan has cited Wilson's work as among his influences for the film, notably Homer's opening line, "Tell me about a complicated man." Granted, it's nothing short of a herculean task to try and fit every last element of Homer's 12,000-line poem into a film that runs just under three hours, despite that IMAX screen size . Nolan does hit the major plot points of The Odyssey 's 20-year chronology the Trojan Horse, Penelope's (Anne Hathaway) sleazy suitors, Odysseus (Matt Damon) and his men running into the cyclops Polythemus (Bill Irwin) , the sirens, the Scylla monster, and the nymph Calypso (Charlize Theron) along the way he leaves many myths deliberately untold. Featured Video For You What It Was Like Shooting One of Oppenheimer s Most Remarkable Moments For one thing, Wilson's translation famously reexamined the role of women in The Odyssey , like the characters of Penelope and Circe. And Nolan's

title: F1 in Belgium: Machine learning algorithms are ruining the sport
author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
body: Formula 1 returned to the Spa-Francorchamps circuit for the Belgium Grand Prix this weekend. This ribbon of tarmac in the Ardennes is one of the sport's historic venues, featuring in the first F1 season way back in 1950. But as yesterday's race showed, the current F1 machinery is utterly ill-suited to this dramatic venue. As we saw at Suzuka in Japan earlier this year, a 2026 F1 battery is able to power the race car's electric motor for only a few seconds a lap; for much of the rest of the lap, the energy-starved cars have less power going to their rear wheels than in the junior F2 feeder series. The best race track in the world For many, including much of the current F1 grid, Spa is considered perhaps the planet's greatest race track. In fact, both Spa and the Belgian Grand Prix predate the F1 world championship, with its first race held in 1922. Back then, the circuit was a 9-mile loop of public roads, taking in the towns of Malmedy and Stavelot before returning to Francorchamps. In the postwar years, the layout shrank slightly to 8.7 miles (14.1 km) and was ferociously fast, with average lap speeds of up to 150 mph (240 km/h)—and ferociously dangerous, given cars with huge amounts of power and minimal concessions to safety. title: Dr. Jill Lepore on why AI backlash is vital for the future
body: Today, I’m talking with Harvard professor and New Yorker staff writer Dr. Jill Lepore about her new book, The Rise and Fall of the Artificial State , which comes out on August 25. Jill is one of the best writers there is at identifying institutional patterns in history, and Decoder is a show about systems, so this book could not possibly be more in our zone. You build systems by measuring things — gathering information, quantifying it as data, and taking action on it. And Jill’s big thesis in the book is that this kind of systemic measurement is really a dehumanizing act, one that flattens human concerns into something, well, artificial. These artificial structures touch everything — not just in the more obvious places like in apps or AI, but all the way up to politics and governance itself, and Jill’s insights here are a telling portrait of the way Big Tech and its billionaire figureheads have managed to break the very idea of the state itself. Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Decoder wherever you get your podcasts. Head here . Not a subscriber? You can sign up here . You’ll hear Jill tell me she wanted to write a “kind of grumpy book,” but in my reading, it’s a pretty dead-ahead look at the last century and a half of technological and social developments and how we got to a place where bots are maybe running everything. Jill’s idea of the artificial state starts with the very idea of quantification, from the first stabs at taking a census thousands of years ago to the invention of modern political polling in the 1930s. But where sh

title: The first trailer for Avengers: Doomsday is finally here
author: Jennifer Ouellette
body: With San Diego Comic-Con kicking off later this week, Marvel Studios has released the first official full trailer for Avengers: Doomsday , the penultimate film in the MCU's Phase Six and the end of the so-called "Multiverse Saga." (Spoilers for prior relevant MCU films below.) Marvel Studios originally planned to build its Phase Six Avengers arc ( The Kang Dynasty ) around Jonathan Majors’ Kang the Conqueror (and associated variants), introduced in Loki and Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania . But then Majors was convicted of domestic violence, and Marvel fired the actor soon after. That meant the studio needed to retool its Phase Six plans, culminating in the announced return of the Russo brothers, who directed four of the Marvel Cinematic Universe’s most successful films, which brought in more than $6 billion at the global box office. Read full article Comments

Instead of battling those elements, stay indoors thanks to this deal. As of July 20, the Peloton Tread is on sale at Amazon for $2,895, marked down from the standard price of $3,295. There's a 21.5-inch HD screen that swivels, front facing speakers, and a generously sized 59-inch-long running belt. Peloton put the speed and incline knobs in a convenient location, making them easy to adjust while mid-workout. The jump button will increase the speed by one and the incline by one percent. Overall, you can select a speed between 1 and 12.5 miles per hour, in increments of .1 miles for plenty of control. It packs an NQ4 AI Gen2 processor that can upscale content to 4K and an extremely thin, glossy OLED HDR panel. The glossy display can be more prone to reflections, but it can also preserve black levels better in bright rooms. title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices
author: Hannah Murphy, Financial Times
body: Nearly half of companies that are targets of a ransomware cyber attack end up paying a ransom to release their data or systems, according to 2025 research from cyber security group Sophos, while the median amount demanded is rising. Globally, some jurisdictions are responding by banning payments to hackers. In the UK, for example, the government is advancing plans to prohibit public sector bodies and critical national infrastructure groups—including the National Health Service, local councils and schools—from making payouts. The potential veto comes as ransomware hackers have become more advanced and meticulous in their targeting of companies, particularly vulnerable small and medium-sized businesses, over time. Read full article Comments

body: Electric aviation is still in its infancy, but manufacturers are already looking beyond mere air taxi trips across cities. Today, one of the leading air taxi startups, San Jose-based Archer Aviation, unveiled a new aircraft it co-developed with defense technology company Anduril - though the platform is designed for both military and commercial uses, the companies said. The new aircraft looks like a cross between Archer's Midnight air taxi and a more established military vertical takeoff and landing aircraft like a V-22 Osprey. title: Avengers: Doomsday trailer: Robert Downey Jr. and Chris Evans return for a star-stuffed superhero epic
body: Avengers: Doomsday is primed to be the biggest superhero movie yet. Marvel Entertainment has dropped a jaw-dropping trailer, and the sheer amount of superheroes in it is dizzying. The Fantastic Four are back, as are Avengers like Ant-Man, Captain America, The Winter Soldier, Thor, and Steve Rogers . Plus, there's Loki, Shang-Chi , the warriors of Wakanda, including the new Black Panther, and Thunderbolts ' Yelena, John Walker, the Red Guardian, and Ghost. And representing for the X-Men are Professor X, Magneto, Cyclops, Mystique, and Gambit. And of course, Robert Downey Jr., who previously played Tony Stark/Iron Man, is back as Doctor Doom, typically a Fantastic Four foe. Also appearing in the film are Chris Evans, Chris Hemsworth, Pedro Pascal, Paul Rudd, Anthony Mackie, Florence Pugh, Vanessa Kirby, Ebon Moss-Bachrach, Wyatt Russell, Channing Tatum, Simu Liu, Ian McKellen, Tom Hiddleston, James Marsden, Patrick Stewart, Joseph Quinn, Sebastian Stan, David Harbour, Letitia Wright, Lewis Pullman, Kelsey Grammer, Kathryn Newton, Danny Ramirez, Winston Duke, Alan Cumming, Hannah John-Kamen, Rebecca Romijn, Mabel Cadena, and Tenoch Huerta Mejia. Anthony Russo and Joe Russo direct Avengers: Doomsday , which opens in theaters on Dec. 18.

title: The Haylou Watch Hyper packs dual-band GPS and offline maps — pre-order now with a discount
body: SAVE $9.01: Pre-order the new Haylou Watch Hyper for $75.99 (regularly $85) and get dual-band GPS, offline maps, route backtracking, and up to 24 days of battery life. Opens in a new window Credit: Haylou Haylou Watch Hyper $75.99 at Haylou $85 Save $9.01 Get Deal Haylou has officially introduced its latest smartwatch , the Watch Hyper , and it's aiming to make outdoor-ready features more affordable. While many GPS smartwatches with offline maps can cost hundreds of dollars, the Watch Hyper launches at $85, with a $9.01 preorder discount automatically applied at checkout. The company says the Watch Hyper is built for people who split their time between city life and outdoor adventures, combining navigation tools, fitness tracking, and everyday smartwatch features into a single device. One of the Watch Hyper's standout features is its L1 + L5 dual-band GPS, which improves location accuracy whether you're navigating busy city streets or hiking on trails. It also includes offline maps, full-color topographic maps, and route backtracking, so you can find your way even without a cellular connection. The smartwatch features a 1.97-inch AMOLED display with adaptive brightness, making it easy to see your screen in bright outdoor conditions. If fitness is your thing, the Watch Hyper supports more than 170 sports modes and offers features like smart workout detection, VO₂ Max estimates, personalized training goals, and workout insights powered by Haylou's Sevier motion algorithm. Haylou says the Watch Hyper can last up to 24 days on a single charge, or up to 16 hours with conti

title: English footballer Wayne Rooneys blunt reaction to World Cup halftime show goes viral
body: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final on Sunday was something of a first for the tournament, in that it featured a halftime show similar to the kind you typically associate with the Super Bowl . Coldplay's Chris Martin curated the MetLife Stadium show with Global Citizen and he certainly went all out, bringing together performances from the likes of Shakira, BTS, Justin Bieber, and Burna Boy , a cameo from Ted Lasso (Jason Sudeikis) himself, and a special appearance from the Muppets. SEE ALSO: T-Mobile is handing over the Apple iPhone 17 for free how to claim yours Needless to say the internet reacted , and there was plenty of excitement from most corners. But not all. Watching on from the BBC Sport commentary box, it's fair to say former England footballer-turned-pundit Wayne Rooney was less than impressed with the spectacle. This Tweet is currently unavailable. It might be loading or has been removed. Responding to host Gabby Logan's question to name his favorite bit of the halftime show, Rooney remained straight-faced as he gave the following response: "I'll be honest, mine was when it finished. I like a lot of them artists, but I thought it was crap." That clip has now been shared thousands of times on X. You certainly can't fault his honesty. body: TL;DR: Samsung's new 990 SSD is now available with 1TB ($269.99) and 2TB ($529.99) . The PCIe Gen 4 NVMe drive promises read speeds of up to 7,250 MB/s and write speeds of up to 6,450 MB/s. Opens in a new window Credit: Samsung Samsung 990 SSD $269.99 at Amazon Shop Now We know upgrading your PC hasn't been exactly cheap lately. Between RAMageddon and electronics price hikes across the board, everything from SSDs to laptops have become more expensive in 2026. But if you're looking to add more storage, Samsung has a new option on the table. Samsung has officially launched the Samsung 990 SSD, a PCIe Gen 4 NVMe drive built for everyday users who want fast, responsive storage for gaming, creative work, and daily computing. Available now in 1TB ($269.99) and 2TB ($529.99) capacities, the new drive focuses on delivering high performance while using less power than Samsung's flagship 990 Pro. What does the Samsung 990 SSD offer? The Samsung 990 SSD is designed to speed up everyday tasks, whether you're loading games, moving large files, or managing creative projects. It delivers sequential read speeds of up to 7,250 MB/s and write speeds of up to 6,450 MB/s, helping reduce load times, speed up installations, and transfer high-resolution photos and videos quickly. Samsung says the drive reaches random read and write speeds of up to 850K and 1,200K IOPS, which can help keep everyday workloads running smoothly and responsively. The new drive is positioned as an easy upgrade for existing PCs or a dependable option for new desktop builds. More speed without the price tag Beyond raw sp

title: BTS met IShowSpeed at the World Cup final and its everything
body: BTS and IShowSpeed met each other in the hallway at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final and the video is pure glee with added backflip. The South Korean superstars performed in the first ever World Cup halftime show on Sunday, during the final match between victors Spain and runners up Argentina. In a delightful exchange on the day, BTS members RM, Jin, SUGA, j-hope, Jimin, V, and Jung Kook ran into the YouTube megastar (and Mashable 101 alumnus ) in the halls of MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. SEE ALSO: English footballer Wayne Rooney's blunt reaction to World Cup halftime show goes viral In a video posted to Instagram by IShowSpeed, it's hard to tell who is fanning out harder. View this post on Instagram Speed's backflip? So casual. And there's a lot of "big fan" exclamations exchanged all round. It's extremely sweet. "We were just as amazed to meet you🙌" wrote BTS' V in the comments. J-Hope and RM also added emoji under the video. During the World Cup halftime show , BTS performed their 2020 Grammy nominated, chart-topping, YouTube-smashing single "Dynamite," the group's first number one in America. In a performance curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin and Global Citizen, BTS shared the lineup with Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira, Burna Boy, the Muppets, and more, including appearances from football legends Ronaldo and Ronaldinho and Ted Lasso 's Jason Sudeikis and Brendan Hunt in character. title: Apple Music will now cost you more
body: Apple has increased the subscription price of Apple Music in the U.S., but that's not where the price hikes end. An Apple Music Individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, up from $10.99. Student plans now cost $6.99, up from $5.99. And if you want to get a Family plan, that'll be $19.99, up from $16.99. Europeans get the exact same numbers, only in euros: Individual plans are now 11.99, Student plans are 6.99, and a Family plan is 19.99. In the UK, the Individual plan now costs 11.99, while the Family plan is 19.99. New subscribers will have to pay the new prices right away; existing subscribers will get a notification soon and will be billed the new prices at their next billing cycle. In a statement to Music Business Worldwide , Apple said the price hikes are a result of "rising licensing costs." SEE ALSO: Apple price increases arrive: How much MacBooks and iPads will cost you now Apple One plans have also gotten pricier. While the basic plan still has the same price, the Family plan now costs $27.95 (up from $25.95), while the Premier plan costs $39.95 (up from $37.95). There's more bad news for consumers, although it's scattered throughout different markets. As MacRumors noticed , iPhone prices have gone up roughly 8-10 percent in Japan, and iCloud+ prices have gone up quite a bit (from 11 to 55 percent) in 8 countries: Nigeria, Turkey, Vietnam, Japan, Egypt, New Zealand, the Philippines, and Indonesia. The news comes about a month after Apple increased the prices of numerous products in its lineup, mostly due to the memory chip supply crunch.

title: Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup halftime show with Shakira, BTS, and Justin Bieber
body: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final kicked off on Sunday evening at MetLife Stadium, New Jersey, with Argentina and Spain battling it out for the championship title. This year's tournament was a little different though, with FIFA staging its first halftime show in the competition's 96-year history. SEE ALSO: The internet reacts to the first-ever World Cup Halftime show Curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin, the FIFA World Cup halftime show was an 11-minute gauntlet of musicians from across the globe, including Shakira , BTS , Justin Bieber , and Burna Boy. The Muppets also played an instrumental rendition of The White Stripes' "Seven Nation Army" with the New York Philharmonic orchestra. Madonna opened the show with a mashup of "Music" and "Danceteria," with Brazilian soccer greats Ronaldo and Ronaldinho appearing alongside her, while K-pop phenomenon BTS performed a truncated version of their English-language hit "Dynamite." Ted Lasso actors Jason Sudeikis and Brendan Hunt then made cameos, respectively playing the title character and Coach Beard from the feel-good football series, and giving Justin Bieber a pep talk before he slowed things down with an acoustic performance of "Everything Hallelujah." Shakira and Burna Boy followed with the 2026 FIFA World Cup's official anthem "Dai Dai," delivering a jarring change of mood, before before Coldplay, the Muppets, and the PS22 Chorus children's choir closed out the halftime show by debuting the band's new song "We Dance." The full performance is available now on FIFA's official YouTube channel . Unfortunately, despite the star-studded

title: Hurdle hints and answers for July 20, 2026
body: If you like playing daily word games like Wordle , then Hurdle is a great game to add to your routine. There are five rounds to the game. The first round sees you trying to guess the word, with correct, misplaced, and incorrect letters shown in each guess. If you guess the correct answer, it'll take you to the next hurdle, providing the answer to the last hurdle as your first guess. This can give you several clues or none, depending on the words. For the final hurdle, every correct answer from previous hurdles is shown, with correct and misplaced letters clearly shown. An important note is that the number of times a letter is highlighted from previous guesses does necessarily indicate the number of times that letter appears in the final hurdle. Mashable 101 Fan Fave: Nominate your favorite creators today If you find yourself stuck at any step of today's Hurdle, don't worry! We have you covered. title: Moon phase today: What the Moon will look like on July 20
body: Today is International Moon Day, a day dedicated to celebrating the role the Moon has played in our understanding of space. Held each year on July 20, the event commemorates the historic Apollo 11 mission and the first time humans set foot on the Moon. Keep reading to see what you can spot on the Moon's surface tonight. What is today s Moon phase? As of Monday, July 20, NASA's Daily Moon Guide tracker tells us the Moon phase is in Waxing Crescent phase, with 38% of its surface visible. You won't need any visual aids to be able to spot the Mares Crisium and Fecunditatis. Binoculars will also help you to see the Endymion Crater, Posidonius Crater, and the Mare Nectaris. A telescope will of course widen your visibility, with areas like the Apollo 17 and 11 landing spot, the perfect way to celebrate International Moon Day. When is the next Full Moon? The next Full Moon will take place on July 29. What are Moon phases? The Moon completes one full cycle around Earth in about 29.5 days, moving through eight different phases along the way, NASA explains. Although the same side of the Moon always faces our planet, the amount of sunlight reflecting off its surface changes as it travels around Earth. This changing angle of illumination is what makes the Moon appear to transform throughout the month, from a thin crescent to a half-lit Moon and eventually a fully illuminated Full Moon, before starting the cycle again. New Moon - The Moon is between Earth and the sun, so the side we see is dark (in other words, it's invisible to the eye). Waxing Crescent - A small sliver of light appears

title: NYT Pips hints, answers for July 20, 2026
body: Welcome to your guide to Pips, the latest game in the New York Times catalogue. Released in August 2025, Pips puts a unique spin on dominoes, creating a fun single-player experience that could become your next daily gaming habit. Currently, if you're stuck, the game only offers to reveal the entire puzzle, forcing you to move on to the next difficulty level and start over. However, we have you covered! Below are piecemeal answers that will serve as hints so that you can find your way through each difficulty level. How to play Pips If you've ever played dominoes, you'll have a passing familiarity with how Pips is played. As we've shared in our previous hints stories for Pips, the tiles, like dominoes, are placed vertically or horizontally and connect with each other. The main difference between a traditional game of dominoes and Pips is the color-coded conditions you have to address. The touching tiles don't necessarily have to match. SEE ALSO: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 20, 2026 The conditions you have to meet are specific to the color-coded spaces. For example, if it provides a single number, every side of a tile in that space must add up to the number provided. It is possible and common for only half a tile to be within a color-coded space. Here are common examples you'll run into across the difficulty levels: Number : All the pips in this space must add up to the number. Equal : Every domino half in this space must be the same number of pips. Not Equal : Every domino half in this space must have a completely different number of pips. Less than : Every domino ha

title: The Vampire Lestat: Burning questions following The Failures finale
body: As promised from the first glimpse of the aspiring rock star Brat Prince, The Vampire Lestat has been a wild ride. The third season of Rolin Jones' Interview with the Vampire was a chaotic odyssey led by Lestat de Lioncourt ( Sam Reid ), as he reconnected with his long-lost love Louis de Pointe du Lac (Jacob Anderson), antagonized his documentarian Daniel Molloy (Eric Bogosian), faced the wrath of his departed daughter Claudia (Delainey Hayles), taunted his ex Armand (Assad Zaman), hooked up with his fledgling/mom Sofia/Gabriella (Jennifer Ehle), and awakened Akasha, aka the Queen of the Damned (Sheila Atim). However, the finale episode titled "The Failures," after Lestat's recorded telling of his life and times didn't put to rest all of the many, juicy threads unearthed. So, here's what we expect to see more of in Season 2. How did Nicki die? In interviews with Mashable, Reid teased that this season was largely about Lestat running from his past trauma rather than confronting it. In episode 3, Lestat told Daniel via a telepathy trick about how Armand threw Nicki into a fire, and the horrid "O" sound his lover made as he perished. But in the finale, that same moan returns as Lestat remembers seeing the alleged witches burned when he was just a boy. Reid told Mashable , "And then you see [Lestat] reprocess that memory as his own memory, watching these women die, and understanding for the first time: Yes, Gabriella has abused him, and has assaulted him throughout his whole life." This means Nicki's death which was notably not shown in the series didn't go down exactly as Lest

title: Sam Reid reveals the major obstacles of The Vampire Lestat: One Night Only – LIVE
body: With The Vampire Lestat, Interview with a Vampire fans witnessed Lestat de Lioncourt (Sam Reid) pursue his rock star ambitions in a bloody and wild tour across North America. In real life, Reid got a taste of the attention his character craves with The Vampire Lestat: One Night Only - LIVE . As advertised, Reid performed songs from The Vampire Lestat written by Daniel Hart before a live audience for one night only. Mashable was there. In my review of the event , I wrote, "Reid's onstage persona evoked David Bowie, Mick Jagger, and Steven Tyler, as he crooned, strutted, flipped his hair, flung a scarf, and generally oozed sex appeal with a crooked grin." But, what was it like for the man behind the mic? Trickier than you might have dreamed. In my latest interview with Reid, he dug into The Vampire Lestat 's finale , where the series might go next , and what it was like for him to take to the stage in character as Lestat. "It's funny," Reid said, "These things it's very work for me, you know what I mean? I am not a touring musician. I'm an actor, and so I'm playing a character. I'm going out there, promoting a show. It was wonderful to be able to play that music with those musicians, namely Daniel Hart. I'm really proud of his music, and I think he worked his ass off in this season. So I'm really proud to share it." Reid went on: "It's quite easy to play Lestat for me, you know. So there was a level of ease about it, because I'm not having to go out and share my own personal music and bear my soul." This distance allowed Reid to focus on his character over any nerves about pe

title: Craving The Vampire Lestat Season 2? Sam Reid gave us some idea of whats coming next.
body: Well, when it came to predicting how Season 1 of The Vampire Lestat would end, I was way, way off. Show creator Rolin Jones went way off-book from Anne Rice's source material, crafting a fascinating, mind-bending finale. While the decapitated Louis de Pointe du Lac (Jacob Anderson) had to face his ex-lover Armand (Assad Zaman), Lestat de Lioncourt ( Sam Reid ) had to face the long table and its Failure Finders. Thankfully, both survived their brushes with death. But in the finale's final scene, Lestat reveals the ravaged world sparked by the concert that the episode skipped over. We've got burning questions. But in an interview with Mashable, Reid dropped some hints on what's next for the Interview with the Vampire series. What's coming up in The Vampire Lestat Season 2? Over a video call with me, Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko, Reid noted that the next season of Interview with a Vampire will move beyond Anne Rice's second novel, for which this season was named, The Vampire Lestat . "The way that [the show's writers] pull from the books," Reid explained, "we're not necessarily just doing The Vampire Lestat . We're looking at multiple books here in the overall arc particularly those next three books." Those would be The Queen of the Damned, The Tale of the Body Thief, and Memnoch the Devil. In the first of these, the central narrator conceit is abandoned, as Rice took on the perspective of a variety of characters, including Baby Jenks . The focus of the overall story is the rise of Akasha (Sheila Atim) and the devastation she wreaks upon the world of mortals and

title: The Vampire Lestat finale: Sam Reid on witches burning and poor Nicki
body: Early on in The Vampire Lestat 's first season, the eponymous anti-hero mentions a formatively traumatic moment that we finally see in full in the finale, titled "The Failures." When Lestat de Lioncourt (Sam Reid) was just a boy, his mother, Gabriella (Jennifer Ehle), took him to see their neighbors burn alleged witches alive. In the final episode of this season, Lestat finally stops deflecting about what this moment meant. At the long table, before the Failure Finders , he confronts his resentment at his mother for how this execution impacted him for the next 200-plus years. In a post-mortem interview, Sam Reid told Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko what that scene means for The Vampire Lestat and how it connects to the final moments of Nicolas de Lenfent (Joseph Potter). What did the witches burning mean to Lestat? Featured Video For You 'The Vampire Lestat's Sam Reid on Magnus, Claudia, Sofia, and more In episode 7 of The Vampire Lestat , the version of Gabriella at the long table argues that she took the young child Lestat to witness the witches burn so that he would see his neighbors for the vicious and ignorant "cabbages" they are. She wanted him to be motivated to leave this terrible place for something better. Asked about this sequence, Reid told Mashable, "That was one of the main conversations I had with [showrunner] Rolin [Jones] about the whole Lestat journey this season, was getting to the core of him, [which] was this existential crisis that he had when he was a child." "You know, he [initially] puts it on Nicki, because he cannot deal with that," Re

title: The Vampire Lestat finale: Sam Reid reveals why Paul is back
body: When Sam Reid first sat down with Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko, they dug into the first three episodes of The Vampire Lestat , focusing on the Brat Prince's perspective on his makers and f emale fledglings . Now, in a post-mortem interview, he's dug into the season's mind-snapping finale, the Failure Finders, what's next for Lestat , and why Paul de Pointe du Lac (Steven G. Norfleet) makes a surprise appearance. In episode 7, titled "The Failures," Lestat faces the long table. There, after facing down those he loves and loathes most, he turns it into a stage, where he performs for his long line of victims. But as his time is running out, and it seems his death is coming, it's Louis' long-gone brother Paul who died by his own volition and not by Lestat's fangs who comes to share a quiet moment with him. Reid reflected on what this scene means to Lestat. Why does Paul show up at the long table? Featured Video For You 'The Vampire Lestat's Sam Reid on Magnus, Claudia, Sofia, and more Reid says of this sequence, "These people at the concert are the ones who he has traumatized in their life, or he has killed. But Paul represents the reason why Louis can't say 'I love you' to him." In the scene, Paul seems to come from the afterlife as Claudia did, but with no wrath to meet Lestat, and offer a word about what he's realized about his brother. In death (or near it), the religious zealot and the Brat Prince are able to empathize with Louis in a way they couldn't before. "The reason why [Lestat] can't get the validation that he needs from the person he wants it from th

title: Sam Reid on The Vampire Lestats finale and the Failure Finders
body: After much fucking about, Lestat hit his find out era. In episode 7, titled "The Failures," while a decapitated Louis de Pointe du Lac (Jacob Anderson) is tortured by a bitter Armand (Assad Zaman), Lestat is forced to face his past. We finally see the long table teased all season long in the opening credits. Earlier this year, Reid came by Mashable's studio for a Say More interview , where he talked Claudia , Magnus , and much more. Now, he and Mashable Entertainment Editor Kristy Puchko reconnected for a postmortem of the Brat Prince's finale. Who are the Failure Finders at the long table? At the head of the table sits Lestat's maker, Magnus (Damien Atkins). Then around the table, his fledglings, Nicolas "Nicki" de Lenfent (Joseph Potter), Louis, Gabriella (Jennifer Ehle), Antoinette (Maura Grace), and his bandmate TC (Sarah Swire). But no Claudia (Bailey Bass). As Lestat's severed head is dying, who are these figures? Are they ghosts of those he's loved? But how so, when some are still alive? Are they the voices in his head? Something supernatural from the savage garden? "I don't think [Lestat] knows." Reid told Mashable, "But he knows that they are coming. The whole season's been set up for this sequence. [In episode 1] Baby Jenks says, 'You know, they're coming for you.' Who are they? And then Magnus in the car [in episode 3] is like, 'At the long table, we eat your soul.' So, he knows that they are coming the long table is coming." He went on, "I think when Lestat arrives there [at the long table], because [he is] a supernatural being, there's a lot of space to accept

title: House of the Dragon Season 3, episode 5s shocking ending is way different in the book
body: Just two episodes after Queen Rhaenyra Targaryen (Emma D'Arcy) threw a nauseating rat banquet , House of the Dragon upped its gross food game with another disgusting feast. The feast shows up in Season 3, episode 5, which reveals that Ormund Hightower (James Norton) has been bankrolling the murders of Gold Cloaks across King's Landing. By the episode's end, Daemon Targaryen (Matt Smith) and Ser Luthor Largent (Tom Cullen) have killed one man who was involved in the killings, and who claims he was acting alone. However, that turns out not to the case, because by the episode's end, nine other Gold Cloaks are dead, including Luthor. SEE ALSO: 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 family tree: How the Targaryens, Hightowers, and Velaryons connect Daemon discovers these corpses posed in a horrifying tableau. The deceased Gold Cloaks sit around a long table, their innards splayed out among tankards and candles. Above them, someone has painted the words "a feast for traitors" in blood. It's a disgusting sequence, a cross between "The Last Supper" and body horror. It's also not in House of the Dragon 's source material, George R.R. Martin's Fire and Blood at least, not in this exact way. For starters, the Gold Cloak killing spree is a show-only addition, as are all of Ormund's plans to crown Daeron Targaryen (Benjamin Evan Ainsworth). However, the corpse feast does take place, just in a very different setting. In Fire and Blood , as Ser Criston Cole (Fabien Frankel) and his men march south through the riverlands, his scouts come across a similar corpse feast. Pro-Rhaenyra forces staged a

title: Wordle today: Answer, hints for July 20, 2026
body: Today's Wordle answer should be easy to solve if you love the water. If you just want to be told today's word, you can jump to the bottom of this article for today's Wordle solution revealed. But if you'd rather solve it yourself, keep reading for some clues, tips, and strategies to assist you. SEE ALSO: Mahjong, Sudoku, free crossword, and more: Play games on Mashable SEE ALSO: NYT Connections hints today: Clues, answers for July 20, 2026 Where did Wordle come from? Originally created by engineer Josh Wardle as a gift for his partner, Wordle rapidly spread to become an international phenomenon, with thousands of people around the globe playing every day. Alternate Wordle versions created by fans also sprang up, including battle royale Squabble , music identification game Heardle , and variations like Dordle and Quordle that make you guess multiple words at once . Wordle eventually became so popular that it was purchased by the New York Times , and TikTok creators even livestream themselves playing . What's the best Wordle starting word? The best Wordle starting word is the one that speaks to you. But if you prefer to be strategic in your approach, we have a few ideas to help you pick a word that might help you find the solution faster. One tip is to select a word that includes at least two different vowels, plus some common consonants like S, T, R, or N. What happened to the Wordle archive?
